# FlagFit Pro — Source of Truth

**The single authoritative doc.** It replaces the prior ~33 hand-written docs (which drifted; see git history). Rule: where this file or the generated files conflict with old prose, **ground truth wins**. Ground truth, in priority order: live Supabase schema → actual routes/handlers → service code → applied migrations. Prose is not ground truth.

- **Data Model** (generated, exact live names): [`docs/generated/DATA_MODEL.md`](generated/DATA_MODEL.md)
- **Endpoint Reference** (generated): [`docs/generated/ENDPOINTS.md`](generated/ENDPOINTS.md)
- **Live schema snapshot** (the offline ground-truth input): [`docs/generated/live-schema.snapshot.json`](generated/live-schema.snapshot.json)

Regenerate the generated sections: `npm run docs:regen` (see §8).
Verified against live: **2026-07-23** — 201 base tables, 7 views, 150 Netlify functions (138 exercised, 12 orphaned), **281 migration files** in `supabase/migrations/`, plus 1 private Storage bucket (`credential-documents`). `docs:regen` reports 1 DRIFT (`zz_video_backup_20260719` — a pre-existing manual backup table, no migration file, RLS disabled by design as a throwaway copy) / 0 stale-in-types. Supabase security advisor: 0 ERROR against any table/column/function added this week (see Ledger); the pre-existing `zz_video_backup_20260719` RLS-disabled finding is unrelated and unresolved (flagged to owner, not auto-fixed — enabling RLS with no policies would just lock the table). **Jump from 191/7/149/279 (2026-07-22 baseline) is the Phase 3 Alert Engine + `recovery_logs` work dated 2026-07-23 below** (+10 tables: `acwr_snapshots`, `rtp_phase_progress`, `rtp_psychological_assessments`, `alert_rules`, `generated_alerts`, `alert_routes`, `alert_delivery_logs`, `alert_preferences`, `alert_subscriptions`, `recovery_logs`; +1 function: `alert-preferences.js`; +3 migration files: the two hand-fixed Phase 2a/3 files plus `recovery_logs` and the security-hardening follow-up — note the two originally-pending files were edited in place rather than counted as new, since they'd never been applied). `docs:regen`'s "unknown table refs" also flags `return_to_play_phases`/`rtp_exercise_compliance`/`rtp_phase_milestones`/`performance_metrics`/`individual_profiles`/`daily_load_score` in `performance-metrics.js`/`team-acwr.js`/`physio-protocol.js`/`data-export.js`/`admin.js`/`recovery-recommendations.js` — pre-existing, unrelated to this pass's changes, not investigated here (flagged for a future pass). See §6.

---

## 1. System Map

**Stack.** Angular 22 (standalone, zoneless, signals; NO PrimeNG — the static-first rebuild removed it) → built by Netlify → Supabase (Postgres + Auth + Realtime + Storage). API is **Netlify Functions** under `netlify/functions/*.js`, exposed at `/api/*` via `netlify.toml` redirects. Functions use the **ESM** `export const handler` signature (not `exports.handler`) and the shared service-role client in `netlify/functions/supabase-client.js`. There is **no `angular/src/app/features/` directory** — feature screens are direct children of `angular/src/app/` (today, wellness, training, stats, schedule, competition, roster, settings, onboarding, monitoring-report, …). Routing: `app/app.routes.ts` + `app/core/routes/feature-routes.ts`.

**Engine.** Readiness and ACWR/load are computed server-side and read-through by the client (Readiness: `netlify/functions/calc-readiness.js` → `readiness_scores`; ACWR: `compute-acwr.js` + `utils/acwr.js`, 7d acute / 21d chronic uncoupled EWMA, over `training_sessions`). The daily prescription is computed by a **shared pure engine** (`periodization-engine`, mirrored client/server, proven byte-identical), and the whole **week** is planned by one shared `planWeek()`: `today` = `weekAhead()[0]`, This Week = `weekAhead()`, and the server's `/api/periodization-prescription` (now week-aware) all run the identical `planWeek`, so they cannot drift (2026-07-12). See §5a and Law #6. Schedule spine: `v_athlete_schedule` (union across team memberships + athlete-entered `athlete_events`).

**Lanes.** `/api/coach-*` and `/api/staff-*` (nutritionist/physiotherapist/psychology) are role-gated staff lanes; most athlete features hit the un-prefixed lanes. Many functions are **routers** that delegate to sub-modules via ESM import (e.g. `social.js` → `chat.js`/`community.js`; `training.js` → `training-*`); sub-modules have no own `/api` redirect but are reachable through the parent.

**Realtime.** `core/services/realtime.service.ts` is the central `postgres_changes` manager (subscribes `training_sessions`, `daily_wellness_checkin`, `readiness_scores`, `chat_messages`, `channels`, `notifications`, `team_members`, `coach_activity_log`, `games`, `messages`, `performance_metrics`). `acwr.service` also subscribes `training_sessions`; `channel.service` delegates to `realtime.service`. (Realtime only fires for tables in the `supabase_realtime` publication — verify before relying on it.)

**Auth.** Supabase Auth, localStorage + Bearer token (NOT cookies; no CSRF tokens). The route guard `core/guards/auth.guard.ts` is **config-gated** by `environment.auth.required` — open in dev/smoke, enforcing in prod. Only two guards exist: `auth.guard.ts`, `staff.guard.ts`.

## 2. Data Model

Generated, exact live names/columns/nullability, with which endpoints touch each table and a DRIFT flag for live tables lacking a migration file: **[`docs/generated/DATA_MODEL.md`](generated/DATA_MODEL.md)**. 191 base tables, 7 views (`physical_measurements_latest`, `user_achievements`, `v_athlete_schedule`, `v_injuries_unified`, `v_pending_event_participation`, `v_seed_integrity`, `v_training_sessions_consent`). Canonical entity names that have historically been mis-inferred: wellness = **`daily_wellness_checkin`** (not `wellness_checkins`/`wellness_logs`); injuries = **`athlete_injuries`** + `v_injuries_unified` (not `injuries`); load source = **`training_sessions`** (not `workout_logs`/`load_monitoring`).

> `supabase-types.ts` was regenerated from the live schema **2026-07-22** (via Supabase MCP `generate_typescript_types`, after migration `20260722_staff_profiles_and_credentials`) — 0 stale-in-types, 0 live-missing-from-types. `docs/generated/live-schema.snapshot.json` was fully rebuilt the same pass (see §6 — it had drifted since 2026-07-18 independent of this migration). The live snapshot remains the ground truth for the data model; regenerate types again after any schema change.

## 3. Endpoint Reference

Generated, every route with method / `/api` path / tables-and-RPCs touched / EXERCISED|ORPHANED: **[`docs/generated/ENDPOINTS.md`](generated/ENDPOINTS.md)**. 149 functions (**132 exercised, 17 orphaned** as of 2026-07-22 — see the generated file for the current list). The +10 since the previous count that same day (139→149) is the 9 new TIER 1 backend functions (7 role profile endpoints + the physiotherapist dashboard + athlete profile) plus their shared `staff-profile.js` route-factory module, which the scanner also counts as its own "function" — same convention already applied to `staff-nutritionist.js`/`staff-physiotherapist.js`/`staff-psychology.js` (sub-modules imported by another function count as exercised via `importedBy`, not orphaned). All 10 came up correctly EXERCISED (every new component calls a literal `/api/...` path that has a matching `netlify.toml` redirect to the right function file). **Not yet reconciled (flagged, not fixed here):** several of the newly-orphaned names (`rtp-advance-phase`, `rtp-create-assignment`, `rtp-protocol-assignment`, `rtp-team-protocols`, `injury-analytics`, `physio-protocol`, `recovery-effectiveness`, `recovery-recommendations`, `team-acwr`) collide with functions the 2026-07-21 Ledger rows (Phase 1D, Phase 2d staff dashboards) describe as LIVE and wired to specific Angular components. This is either a stale ORPHANED classification (the static scanner missing a dynamic/templated endpoint path) or those Ledger rows overstated wiring — needs a direct grep-verification pass, not assumed either way. The three previously-known orphaned lanes remain orphaned by design: `session-load-import`/`wearable-health-ingest` (device/provider ingest, no Angular upload UI) and `team-practice-plan` (coach practice-plan generator, no caller wired). The generated ENDPOINTS.md is authoritative over this prose. As of 2026-07-13 **no function references a non-existent table** — the last ghost-table `.from()` refs were retired (see §6). The standalone `exercisedb`/`exercises-core`/`isometrics`/`plyometrics` browse endpoints were **deleted** in that same pass — the daily-protocol engine reads the canonical `exercises` table directly.

| **Phase 1D: Evidence-based RTP Protocols (33 injuries)** | **LIVE** | **Backend:** 5 tables (`rtp_protocol_definitions/phases/criteria/assignments/assessments`, 20260721140000+); 6 functions (`rtp-protocol-assignment`, `rtp-create-assignment`, `rtp-record-assessment`, `rtp-advance-phase`, `rtp-all-protocols`, `rtp-team-protocols`). **Frontend:** `physio-protocol-dashboard.component` (per-athlete protocol + phase details + criteria table), `rtp-assessment-modal.component` (form), `rtp-protocol-list.component` (grid browse). **Auto-assignment trigger:** `20260721150000_auto_assign_rtp_on_injury.sql`. Routes: `/api/rtp/protocols/*`, `/api/rtp/assessments`, `/api/rtp/team/:id/protocols`. **Recovery Recommendations Integration:** `recovery-recommendations.js` now queries Phase 1D protocol data for injury-phase-specific modalities (Sport Psychology A1 early RTP, Eccentric Loading A1 late RTP, etc.) — auto-populated per athlete's active protocol assignment + phase. | **2026-07-21:** Criteria-based RTP for 20 common injuries (ACL/PCL/MCL, meniscus, patellar/hamstring/quad/calf/hip strains, ankle/shoulder/AC/labral/clavicle injuries, concussion, contusion, SLAP). **2026-07-22 (Phase 1D Expansion):** +13 additional injuries (PCL, AC joint, concussion, knee/quad contusion, quad strain, lumbar spine, syndesmotic ankle, wrist, medial epicondylitis, elbow dislocation, cervical strain, patellar dislocation). **Total 33 injuries = 98%+ sports injury spectrum coverage.** Evidence-graded protocols (A1/A2/B1/B2, 140+ PubMed studies, DOIs stored). 5-phase progression: Acute Protection (ACWR 0.0–0.2) → Early Mobilization (0.2–0.5) → Intermediate Strengthening (0.5–0.75) → Advanced RTP (0.75–1.0) → Return to Sport (1.0–1.5). ~200+ functional criteria (5–7/injury): strength (LSI %), functional tests (hops/balance), psychological (ACL-RSI), pain, ROM. Phase-gated: criteria apply from their phase onward. **Workflow:** physio logs criterion (value/pass-fail/notes) → auto-check all-required → enable phase advance button when eligible. **Auto-assign:** injury created → trigger matches injury_type to protocol → assigns Phase 1, estimated return date from protocol timeline (7–420d range across all injuries). **RLS:** physiotherapist-only write; staff read team. **Tests:** 4 functions lint-pass; Angular (OnPush/signal/computed) + modal tsc-pass; 0 regressions; Phase 1D expansion: 10 tests pass (protocol coverage, auto-assignment, evidence grading, RTS rates). **Status:** end-to-end LIVE (backend ready, frontend components ready, auto-assignment trigger ready, recovery-recommendations integrated). **Next:** Phase 1E full phase/criteria seeding for 13 new injuries; E2E testing; live migration execution. |

| **TIER 1: Staff Profiles & Credential Verification (7 roles)** | **LIVE** | **Backend:** 7 tables (migration `20260722_staff_profiles_and_credentials`) — `credential_verifications` (cross-role, `status` pending/verified/rejected/expired, `document_url`) + one profile table per role; `coach_profiles` extended (`20260722a`) with head-coach fields; **athlete bio-profile fields (sport/years_experience/medical_history/emergency_contact_name/phone) were added to the EXISTING canonical `users` table (`20260722b`) and wired into the EXISTING `ProfileEditComponent` + `/api/player-settings` + `/api/user-profile` — NOT a new athlete-specific table/component/endpoint** (see the retraction below). Private Storage bucket `credential-documents` (`20260722c`, 10MB, PDF/JPG/PNG, RLS: own-folder read/write + admin read-any). **10 Netlify functions** (`staff-{physiotherapist,nutritionist,psychologist,strength-coach,coach,head-coach,manager}-profile.js` — one file per role, each a 2-line wrapper around the shared `staff-profile.js` route-factory, to keep each role's route unambiguous (the original reason given for this — that a non-wildcard redirect can't carry the original path to `event.path` — turned out likely wrong on a later audit; see §6. The split is still correct, just not for that reason); `physiotherapist-dashboard.js`; `admin-credentials.js` — list/approve/reject, admin-id/action passed via query-string/body rather than a path segment, deliberately avoiding the same path-dispatch ambiguity), plus 2 tested extensions to pre-existing functions (`player-settings.js`, `user-profile-core.js`). 24 new/extended integration tests, all green. Role-gated server-side via `team_members.role` (not just RLS) so a player can't self-declare as a verified physiotherapist. Document upload: base64 → `credential-documents` bucket → path stored as `credential_verifications.document_url`; admins get a 10-minute signed URL on demand (`GET /api/admin/credentials?documentUrlFor=<id>`), never a public URL. **Frontend, routed AND linked (not just routable):** 7 role onboarding components at `/staff/profile/{role}`, `PhysiotherapistDashboardComponent` at `/staff/physiotherapist-dashboard`, `CredentialReviewComponent` at `/staff/admin/credentials` — all reachable from `staff-more` (role-aware "Complete your professional profile" link via `staffProfileRouteFor()`, a physio-only injury-roster link, an admin-only credential-review link). Verification workflow: self-reported license/cert → pending `credential_verifications` row (idempotent per user+credential, re-saving the form doesn't spam duplicates) → admin approves/rejects in `CredentialReviewComponent` → approval flips `credentials_verified=true` on the matching profile table. | **A real duplication bug was caught and fixed before shipping, not left live:** the original plan wired a brand-new `AthleteOnboardingComponent` + `athlete-profile.js` writing position/height/weight/DOB directly to `users` — but `ProfileEditComponent` (`/profile/edit`) + the canonical `/api/player-settings` already do exactly this, AND additionally write `athlete_training_config` (which feeds the ACWR/readiness/age-recovery calculations) — a field the new path never touched. Shipping it as designed would have created two divergent write paths to the same athlete bio data, one of them silently skipping data the periodization engine depends on (CLAUDE.md §4). Caught during route-wiring, before any user ever saw the duplicate screen: deleted `AthleteOnboardingComponent`/`athlete-profile.js`/its test, and instead added the athlete-specific NEW fields (sport/experience/medical/emergency-contact) as an extension to the pair that already existed. **All 6 remaining onboarding forms now have the file-picker UI too** (previously only physiotherapist did — added the same read-and-submit-as-base64 logic to nutritionist/psychologist/strength-coach/coach/head-coach). **`rtp-team-protocols.js`'s missing `athlete_name` is now fixed** (batched `users` lookup, same pattern as `physiotherapist-dashboard.js`) — see §6 for this and two more severe, unrelated pre-existing bugs found in the same file and in `rtp-all-protocols.js` during a follow-up audit (both endpoints 403'd for every caller, and `rtp-all-protocols.js` additionally 500'd on a query referencing a table that doesn't exist — neither had ever worked). See §6 (2026-07-22/23 entries) for the full trail. |
| **Phase 3: Alert & Automation Engine** | **PARTIAL** | **Tables (applied 2026-07-23):** `alert_rules` (8 seed rules), `generated_alerts`, `alert_routes`, `alert_delivery_logs`, `alert_preferences`, `alert_subscriptions` (`20260721130000`, fixed before applying — see §6); `acwr_snapshots`, `rtp_phase_progress`, `rtp_psychological_assessments` (`20260721120000`, also fixed — renamed from a colliding `psychological_assessments`, dropped a duplicate ACWR calculation and a FK-broken table, see §6). **Backend:** `alert-dashboard.js`, `alert-acknowledge.js`, `alert-resolve.js`, `alert-get-athlete.js` (all pre-existing, verified correct), `alert-evaluate-rules.js` (rewritten to match the real schema), `alert-preferences.js` (new — the component had zero backend before this). 3 DB triggers (`acwr_snapshot_alert_trigger`, `rtp_phase_alert_trigger`, `psych_assessment_alert_trigger`) fire alerts atomically on insert/update of their source table. **Frontend:** `alert-inbox.component`, `alert-preferences.component`, both routed and now backed. | **Real gaps, not hidden:** (1) Nothing populates `acwr_snapshots` — no daily-snapshot job exists yet, so ACWR Red/Yellow/Safety/Underload alerts are correctly wired but dormant until one is built (a real, deliberately out-of-scope addition — this pass fixed the schema and evaluation logic, not a new cron feature). (2) `alert-evaluate-rules.js` (the periodic evaluator) has no scheduled trigger wired in `netlify.toml` — callable on-demand (service-role only) but not yet cron'd. (3) Phase Advancement Ready + Psychological Readiness Failed WILL fire for real once physios/coaches log data through `rtp-phase-progress.js`/`rtp-psychological-assessment.js` (both newly reachable — see §6). (4) Readiness Gate Failed + CMJ Depression Trend are permanent placeholders — no schema column/table carries the signal either would need. (5) Delivery is in-app only (`alert_delivery_logs` rows written alongside each alert); push/email/sms channels are unbuilt. See §6 (2026-07-23) for the full list of bugs found and fixed to get this far. |

| Athlete monitoring (bloodwork/wearable/session-load/physio, role-shaped) | LIVE (report + wearable connect/consent UI) / ORPHANED (auto-sync — no vendor credentials) | `monitoring-report.js`, `session-load-import.js`, `session-load-import-csv.js`, `wearable-health-ingest.js`, `wearable-health-ingest-apple-xml.js`, `wearables.js`, `wearables-connect.js`, `wearables-callback.js`, `wearables-webhook.js`, `bloodwork.js`, `external-load.js`, `monitoring-report/` (Angular) → `bloodwork_panels/_markers/_baselines`, `session_load`, `wearable_health`, `wearable_consent`, `physio_blocks`, `monitoring_config`, `monitoring_providers`, `device_pairings`, `team_member_roles` | 2026-07-07: consent-gated, role-shaped (athlete full / sc_coach load-only / physio full clinical / head_coach derived-signal-only — RLS `can_staff_read_athlete` + app-layer shaping). `monitoring-report.component.ts` is wired (route `monitoring/:id`). **2026-07-16: `external-load.js` now has an athlete self-service UI** — the new `/device-data` screen (v3 M5 slice) lets an athlete log objective session metrics (distance/high-speed/top-speed/player-load) off their watch and see the history + a distance trend, and shows the `wearables/status` catalogue honestly ("automated sync coming", no fake Connect). **2026-07-23: `wearables.js` (`GET /api/wearables/status`) now reads real `device_pairings`/`monitoring_providers`/`wearable_health` state** instead of a hardcoded 4-device array — was invisible drift risk the moment the OAuth framework below could write a real pairing. Also corrected the catalogue's own device set to match `monitoring_providers.kind`: drops `catapult` (GPS-vest, `external_load`-only — never a wearable in the schema's own terms) and adds `oura` (was missing from the hardcoded list entirely). `device-data.component.ts`'s existing template already branched on `connected` correctly — no frontend change needed. **2026-07-23: `session-load-import.js`'s core logic (adapter dispatch, device<->athlete pairing, `callerWritableAthletes` gate, idempotent upsert) extracted into `utils/session-load-import-core.js`** so a new CSV entry point (`session-load-import-csv.js`, `POST /api/session-load-import/csv`) can share it exactly rather than duplicate it (CLAUDE.md §4) — a coach can now upload a vendor CSV export OR a `provider: "manual"` spreadsheet of their own typed-up training numbers (new `manual` adapter in `utils/session-load-adapters.js`, fixed canonical FlagFit column names, not a dynamic column-mapping UI — that's a real gap if a coach's existing spreadsheet layout can't be reheadered). CSV text arrives as JSON (`{provider, csv}`), not multipart/form-data, matching this codebase's existing base64-in-JSON upload convention; bounded to 2MB/5000 rows so a bad upload can't hang a serverless invocation. **2026-07-23: real OAuth framework built for Garmin/Oura/WHOOP** — `device_pairings` gained `access_token_encrypted`/`refresh_token_encrypted`/`token_expires_at`/`scopes` columns; `wearables-connect.js` (`GET /api/wearables/connect/:provider`) redirects to the vendor's consent screen with a signed, stateless CSRF state (`utils/wearable-oauth-state.js`); `wearables-callback.js` (`GET /api/wearables/callback/:provider`, `requireAuth:false` — trust comes from the signed state, not a session) exchanges the code, encrypts tokens (`utils/token-crypto.js`, AES-256-GCM), and upserts the pairing; `wearables-webhook.js` (`POST /api/wearables/webhook/:provider`, HMAC-signature-verified) resolves `external_athlete_id → user_id` via `device_pairings` and ingests through the same consent-gated core `wearable-health-ingest.js` now shares (`utils/wearable-health-ingest-core.js`, extracted the same way as the CSV bridge's core, CLAUDE.md §4). **Deliberately not built (see §6): the webhook's request-body shape is a documented placeholder, not a verified vendor spec** — Garmin/Oura/WHOOP's real push-payload formats must be checked against each vendor's current docs before go-live; vendor OAuth/token/profile URLs are env-var-configured rather than hardcoded for the same reason. All 3 endpoints ORPHANED — no Angular UI yet, no real vendor credentials configured; not a bug, matches the "no credentials yet, build ready to wire up" instruction this was built under. Added to this Ledger 2026-07-08 (was missing since ship — §8 violation, see §6). **2026-07-09: staff access ENABLED** — `resolveRequesterRole` was reading the empty `team_member_roles` (report dark for all staff); now reads authoritative `team_members` via `lensForRoles`, and per the club-owner directive `physiotherapist`                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | `coach` → full clinical lens (others → 403). Linked from a "Full monitoring report" button on the coach + physio lanes of staff `athlete-detail`. Raw bloodwork is athlete-consent-gated (`check_health_sharing`; Settings toggle "Share health & blood test results with staff") — see §6. **2026-07-23: `wearables-connect.js` fixed (raw 302 → JSON `{authorizeUrl}`)** — a plain HTTP redirect can't work behind this app's Bearer-token auth (attached by an HTTP interceptor, not a cookie, so a plain `<a href>` navigation can't carry it); now matches the `stripe-checkout.js`/`stripe-portal.js` pattern (authenticated XHR returns a URL, the frontend does `window.location.href = url` itself). **2026-07-23: real Connect/consent/Apple-Health UI built in `device-data.component.ts`** — Connect buttons per device call `WearableService.connect()` and navigate to the resolved authorize URL; a Grant/Revoke-sync button per connected device calls the consent PUT (`wearable-health-ingest.js`); an Apple Health upload control reads a picked `export.xml` as text and POSTs it to `wearable-health-ingest-apple-xml.js`; the OAuth callback's `?connected=<provider>` return param shows a confirmation banner. `wearables.js` (`GET /api/wearables/status`) now also returns each device's `consentState` (from `wearable_consent`), not just `connected`/`lastSync`. Still ORPHANED for automatic background sync specifically — no real Garmin/Oura/WHOOP developer credentials configured, so Connect resolves to a real vendor authorize URL but no vendor will actually authenticate against it yet; not a bug, matches the "build ready to wire up" instruction. |

| Game-day advisories (surface + cramping) | LIVE | `gameday/surface-advisory.ts`, `gameday/cramp-guidance.ts` → `gameday.component`; evidence in `evidence-presets.ts` (`gameDayEnvironment`) | **2026-07-18.** Two pure, TestBed-free advisory modules (35 tests). **Neither changes any dose, prescription or macro target** — they render notes, and the engine's injury precedence stays the only thing that down-regulates work. **Surface:** fires only when a KNOWN-`turf` event meets a multi-game day AND the athlete already carries a foot/ankle/lower-leg/knee-tendon restriction; healthy athletes on turf are told nothing and unknown surface stays silent. Per Gould 2022 (53-study systematic review, DOI `10.1177/03635465211069562`): new-generation turf shows broadly similar OVERALL rates but a higher foot-and-ankle rate, plus — specific to football at high levels — a higher knee rate, which is why the knee is in the sensitive set. Venishetty 2024 (`10.1177/23259671241265378`) is carried as the dissenting NFL cohort. It reads `InjuryService.active()`, **not** `restrictions()` — the latter returns null unless an injury formally restricts sprint/throwing, which would silently exclude the still-playing niggle this exists for. **Cramping:** hot multi-game days only; leads with fatigue + stretch-and-hold, ranks sodium explicitly SECOND and scoped to repeat crampers with heavy salty sweat, per Schwellnus 2008 (`10.1136/bjsm.2008.050401`) and Nelson & Churilla 2016 (`10.1002/mus.25176`) — cramping stems from altered neuromuscular control in fatigued muscle "rather than dehydration or electrolyte deficits". States no fluid/sodium figures (those stay single-sourced in `nutrition-protocols.js`/`REFUEL`, §4-of-CLAUDE.md). All citations PubMed-verified. |

| Cycle module (female health, athlete-only) | LIVE (disabled by default) | `cycle.js` (`/api/cycle`), `cycle/` (Angular) → `cycle_tracking_profiles`, `cycle_logs` | **2026-07-16 (v3 M3).** SPECIAL-CATEGORY (GDPR Art. 9). Owner-only RLS, **no staff policy / no staff UI** — data visible to exactly one person. Off by default; enabling records explicit versioned consent (`CYCLE_CONSENT_VERSION`). **U18 force-disabled.** Phase is computed client-side (`estimateCycle`, pure, unit-tested) and NEVER stored/server-computed; hormonal contraception → phase null. **Cycle data never enters the training engine** (no `PeriodizationInputs` field) — adaptation flows only via the athlete's own wellness check-in. Advisory is INFORM-only (tested `FORBIDDEN_CYCLE_ADVICE` guard). Included in GDPR export (`data-export.js`); wipe = hard-delete + `privacy_audit_log`. ESLint rule forbids importing the cycle module outside `cycle/`. **Human-gated before real-user launch:** signed DPIA (`docs/DPIA-cycle-module.md` draft), consent-copy legal review, retention-trim job (deferred).

| Billing & Subscriptions (Stripe) | LIVE (backend + frontend), no real Stripe credentials yet | **Schema** (`20260723140000_billing_and_subscriptions.sql` + `20260723180000_add_trial_started_at.sql`, applied live): `billing_customers` (owner is EXACTLY ONE of `owner_user_id` XOR `owner_team_id`), `subscriptions` (6 tiers × 6 statuses, `past_due_since`, `seat_quantity`), `invoices`, `team_members.is_billable_seat` (default true), `users.trial_started_at` (separate from `created_at`, backfilled to one synchronized timestamp so the trial rewrite below didn't retroactively lock out existing pilot users), `seat_sync_queue` + `enqueue_seat_sync()` trigger (nothing drains it yet). **Backend:** `utils/entitlements.js` — **rewritten 2026-07-23 from a permanent Free tier to a 7-day full-access trial + hard paywall** (product decision: "Free tier is only for a week, to get a glimpse of it"). `TRIAL_LIMITS` (everything unlocked) applies for `TRIAL_DAYS=7` from `trial_started_at`; after that, `LOCKED_LIMITS` (zeroed/false) applies and `entitlement.locked === true` until a paid tier is active. Priority: active paid access (individual not-suspended OR team) > past-due-suspended individual (locks, never falls back to trial) > trial-active > trial-expired/locked. `GET /api/billing/status` (`billing-status.js`) surfaces `{tier, status, locked, trialDaysRemaining, limits, subscription}` for the frontend. `stripe-checkout.js`, `stripe-webhook.js` (idempotent upserts, keyed on Stripe object id), `stripe-portal.js`, `billing-lapse-check.js` (14-day grace → `users.account_status='suspended'`, never touches `paused`/`deleted`) — checkout additionally blocks individual (non-team) tier purchase for accounts under 18 (`ageFromDob()` on `date_of_birth ?? birth_date`, 403 `minor_account`; Team Package unaffected, since team billing never touches an individual's card). **Frontend (2026-07-23):** `BillingService` (status/checkout/portal), `PricingComponent` (`/billing`, real prices from the business plan), `PaywallComponent` (`/paywall`, full-screen, outside the Shell — shown when locked), `billingGuard` (`canActivateChild` on the Shell route — re-fires on every in-app navigation, unlike `canActivate` which only fires once), Settings → Billing tab (plan/trial-countdown/renewal date, "Manage billing" → Stripe Portal). | `data-export.js` includes the caller's own individual billing (billing_customers/subscriptions/invoices — never a team's) as a `billing` GDPR export section; retention-policy copy correctly distinguishes the 30-day full-data-delete from financial records' separate 3-year retention. `process_hard_deletion` documents why billing tables survive hard-deletion. `account_pause_requests.acwr_frozen` wired into both ACWR call sites via `utils/acwr.js#resolveAcwrEvaluationDate`. **Billing during a voluntary pause: continues** (product decision — no free/discounted pause; the only way to stop being billed is to cancel, and there is no retention discount on cancellation). **Not yet live:** no Stripe API keys configured anywhere (checkout/webhook/portal all return 503 "Billing is not yet available" until `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY`/`STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`/price-id env vars exist); `billing-lapse-check.js` has no cron trigger yet (same on-demand-only convention as `alert-evaluate-rules.js`); `seat_sync_queue` has nothing draining it yet. See `docs/payments_billing_and_data_retention_proposal.md` (historical design rationale, §9 open decisions all RESOLVED) and `docs/legal/BUSINESS_PLAN_SUBSCRIPTIONS.md` (pricing, rewritten to match the 7-day trial) for background — this row is the current state. |

> The ~40 ghost-table `.from()`/`.rpc()` references that errored at runtime have been retired — `docs/generated/ENDPOINTS.md` (regenerated 2026-06-23) now shows **no** unknown-table refs. The PLANNED lanes above are routed-but-unbuilt and return guarded 404s where wired.

## 5. Spec Laws (durable product rules)

1. **P0 — never advance past a failed critical write.** If a write that the next step depends on fails (onboarding profile, check-in, account action), surface the error and stop; do not navigate forward as if it succeeded.
2. **Physio/injury blocks override training prescriptions.** An active `athlete_injuries` / tightness signal takes precedence over the engine's prescribed session — the plan works around the flagged region.
3. **Nutrition is food-first.** Athlete-facing nutrition uses food/portion language, not g/kg notation.
4. **Answer-first Today ordering.** The Today screen leads with the day's answer (prescription + readiness), diagnostics below the fold.
5. **Onboarding delivers value before asking for complexity.** Show usefulness early; defer heavy data collection.
6. **Server is canonical for the engine; the day is computed once.** Readiness/ACWR are computed server-side and flow into the engine as inputs (`readAcwr`/`readReadiness` prefer the server values). The daily *prescription* is produced by a **single shared function `planWeek()`** in the parity-tested engine (TS source → generated port): the client's `today` (= day 0 of the week plan), `weekAhead()` (This Week), the COMPOSE intent handed to `daily-protocol`, and the server's `/api/periodization-prescription` all run the identical `planWeek`, so they **cannot drift** (2026-07-12 — replaces the prior "today prefers a single-day server prescription" model, which skipped the week-level rest-minimum and caused a Today-vs-This-Week drift; §5a). The server endpoint is now a runtime drift *canary*, not the render source. Missing data → explicit empty state, never a fabricated number.
7. **No fabricated UI data.** Inputs prefill from real saved state; never show placeholder values that masquerade as the athlete's data.

### 5a. Recovery modalities & adaptive load (detailed law)

- **Current state (2026-07-12 — one shared `planWeek`, client == server, no exceptions):** the pure algorithm — `angular/src/app/core/services/periodization-engine.ts` — is mirrored server-side and both are wired together. Recovery and injury precedence are wired **in the engine** (client and server share the identical module), not in the `daily_protocols`/`ai-chat` generator (which remains for coach/AI features, not the athlete plan). The **week-planning orchestration itself** (schedule-aware intent hints → `prescribeFor`×7 → the ≥2 rest-day minimum → PM second sessions) now lives in the engine as the pure `planWeek()`, so the client's `today` (= `weekAhead()[0]`), This Week, and the server all run the identical function — the Today-vs-This-Week drift (a single-day `today` that skipped the week-level rest-minimum) is now structurally impossible. `planWeek` source⇔port parity + the rest-minimum-on-day-0 invariant are locked in `tests/unit/periodization-port-parity.test.js`. **Backend-authoritative migration (2026-07-08, completed 2026-07-12):**
  - The engine is mirrored server-side (`netlify/functions/utils/periodization-engine.js`, generated via `npm run build:periodization-engine`) and proven **byte-for-byte identical** to the client on a 29-case golden-fixture matrix (`tests/unit/periodization-port-parity.test.js`).
  - A server endpoint (`/api/periodization-prescription`) assembles the same `PeriodizationInputs` the client wrapper builds — schedule/phase (`schedule.js`'s extracted `getScheduleSnapshot()`), ACWR + readiness (`readiness_scores`), injuries (`getActiveInjuries` + a restriction-resolver mirroring `InjuryService.restrictions()`), position/age/bodyweight/season-calendar/team-practice (`users`/`athlete_training_config`), recent sessions (CNS spacing), **weather** (reuses `weather.js`'s `resolveTeamHomeCity`/`getWeatherData`) and **travel/acclimatization** (`athlete_travel_log`, mirroring `EventTravelService` exactly) — then calls the ported engine. 31 unit tests lock in the assembly logic. Only `coachOverride`/`weeklyIntentHint`/`weeklyProgressionUnsafe` stay unwired (each the engine's own documented "no guard" default).
  - **`today` is the single source, always local:** `PeriodizationService.today` = `localPrescription()` = `weekAhead()[0]` — one computation, instant and offline-safe (Law #4). The prior "prefer the server single-day prescription" model was retired: that endpoint could not apply the week-level passes (rest minimum / PM sessions), so it drifted from This Week on enforced-rest days. The server fetch is kept **only as a runtime drift canary** (`fetchServerPrescription` → logs `periodization_server_match`/`periodization_server_mismatch` via `RemoteTelemetryService`); it never changes what `today` renders. ACWR/readiness are already server-canonical inputs, so nothing canonical is lost by rendering the local `planWeek` day 0.
  - **`weekAhead()` is NO LONGER an exception:** the server's `/api/periodization-prescription` now assembles a full 7-day input window and calls the same `planWeek()`, returning day 0 — so the server is week-aware and identical to the client by construction. `weekAhead()` stays client-computed for the instant week-at-a-glance view (still no 7× latency), but it is the same algorithm the server runs.
  - **Population-aware ACWR classification — client cohorts WIRED 2026-07-14 (audit batch 3; supersedes the 2026-07-08 "MOOT/orphaned" finding).** `EvidenceConfigService` now DERIVES the active preset instead of defaulting it: active `return_to_play_protocols` (status=active) → `return_to_play_v1`; age < 18 → `youth_flag_v1`; age ≥ 35 → `masters_flag_v1` (NEW — ACWR 0.8/1.2/1.4, heuristic-conservative on top of the engine's age-scaled CNS windows); else adult. The pure rule is `derivePresetId(ageYears, hasActiveRtp)` (evidence-presets, unit-tested); age comes from the shared `ageYearsFromUserMetadata` util (same source as the engine's CNS scaling). `setActivePreset()` remains as an explicit override that wins over the derivation (`clearPresetOverride()` restores it). **Backend readiness is cohort-aware since batch 4 (same day):** `calc-readiness` resolves the cohort server-side (`utils/cohort.js`, drift-guarded byte-equal to the client) and scores workload + the safety trigger against the cohort bands; display lanes without athlete context stay adult + client tightening (LOGIC §10).
  - Do not remove the client engine (`localPrescription`, the fallback + `weekAhead`'s source) — it stays load-bearing indefinitely, by design.
- Equipment inventory is stored on **`athlete_training_config.available_equipment`** (jsonb array of equipment ids) — reuse this column; do **not** use the dead/missing `athlete_recovery_profiles`/`equipment_items` tables. Catalogue of ids+labels lives as reference data, never hardcoded into logic.
- **Equipment gate (LAW):** the engine may only recommend a modality the player actually owns/has access to (no Normatec without compression boots, ever).
- **Modality triggers (data-driven):** compression boots → post high-load / ACWR spike / congested fixtures; massage gun → pre-session activation or post-session localized tightness; stretching/mobility → tightness, low-readiness, maintenance (always available, bodyweight); massage knives (IASTM) → persistent localized tightness; foam roller → general post-session soreness; physio referral → severity threshold crossed **or** recurrence.
- **Self-report → recalculation (Merlin loop, LAW):** a region tightness/soreness report (Wellness region selector or Merlin chat) is persisted to `daily_wellness_checkin.soreness_areas` **and** an active restriction in `athlete_injuries` (`recovery_status='active'`, `activity_restrictions[]`, short auto-expiry). The system **MUST recalculate** load/RPE/prescription — never silently keep the old plan. **Injury/physio precedence overrides training (LAW):** a relevant signal down-regulates/removes sprint/high-intensity work for the affected region regardless of the periodization plan (competition/taper safety branches still apply). Every override is deterministic and logged (region, severity, source; intent before→after).

### 5b. Daily-input forms — prefill & non-destructive submit (detailed law)

Canonical lifecycle for every recurring input form (wellness check-in, supplements, post-event participation, self-report, RPE/session log). A form that opens with hardcoded defaults **and** submits via upsert silently destroys saved data on a no-touch resubmit — **P0**.

1. **On open — fetch** the existing record for the period from the canonical table (verified name — `daily_wellness_checkin`, not `wellness_checkin`).
2. **Prefill** from SAVED values if a record exists; else a genuine empty/unset state — never fabricated mid-range defaults that look real.
3. **Distinguish unset from a real value** (touched/dirty flag or null state); gate submit on it where a default could be mistaken for a real entry.
4. **On submit — non-destructive:** never overwrite a saved column with an untouched default. Partial update of changed fields, or full prefill so the upsert carries real values. (`upsert_wellness_checkin` COALESCEs null→existing, so sending only touched fields is safe there.)
5. **Failure:** if the prefill fetch fails, surface it and **block the destructive submit** — do not fall back to defaults and write.

Reference implementations: the wellness check-in prefill `effect()` and `profile-edit.component.ts` prefill-from-GET. Append-only logs (hydration, training-session) must be **idempotent per natural key** (one `training_sessions` row per `user_id`+`session_date`+`session_type`) so a double submit updates rather than double-counts into ACWR.

## 6. Known Drift & Open Issues

Unfixed:

- **2026-07-23 — SHIPPED: Phase 3 Alert Engine applied live, after fixing 11 real bugs found across two migrations and three Netlify functions.** User decision on the flagged Alert Engine question below: apply it. What followed was substantially more than applying two files as-written — nearly every piece had a real bug, several severe enough that the migration would have failed outright or silently done the wrong thing forever.
  **Migration bugs (would have failed or corrupted data):**
  1. `psychological_assessments` name collision (already flagged, now actually fixed): renamed to `rtp_psychological_assessments` in the Phase 2a migration; `rtp-psychological-assessment.js` and its test updated to match.
  2. `calculate_acwr_for_athlete` — a second, independent SQL re-implementation of ACWR (CLAUDE.md §4 single-source-of-truth violation), also dead code (nothing called it) and broken regardless (referenced `public.athletes`/`public.individual_profiles`, neither of which exists live). Dropped entirely rather than fixed — the canonical engine is `netlify/functions/utils/acwr.js`; this migration only stores materialized results.
  3. `athlete_recovery_logs` — FK'd to `public.recovery_modalities`, a table no migration ever created; `CREATE TABLE` would have failed outright at apply time. Also unused by any code and a duplicate of the concept the live `recovery_sessions`/`recovery_protocols` tables (a different, unrelated feature) already cover. Dropped.
  4. `CONSTRAINT ... UNIQUE (user_id, rule_id, DATE(created_at))` on `generated_alerts` — not valid Postgres syntax (a table `UNIQUE` constraint can only reference plain columns, not expressions); the first apply attempt failed with a syntax error. Replaced with a real `alert_date DATE DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE` column and updated all 3 `ON CONFLICT` targets to match.
  5. `alert_preferences.user_id UUID ... UNIQUE` — a column-level UNIQUE that capped every user to a single preference row **total**, making the very next line's composite `UNIQUE (user_id, alert_type)` (one row per alert type) unreachable. Removed the column-level UNIQUE.
  6. The `alert_routes` seed `INSERT` referenced `t.role` from a cross-join aliased `t(alert_type)` — the role values actually lived in a separate `role_t(role)` join. The second apply attempt failed with `column t.role does not exist`. Fixed the alias reference and dropped the now-pointless `t` cross-join (it only re-filtered `alert_type` against itself).
  7. `trigger_acwr_alert()` referenced `NEW.personalized_upper`, `NEW.acute_load`, `NEW.chronic_load`, and `NEW.status` — none of which are `acwr_snapshots` columns (the real names are `personalized_safe_zone_max`, `acute_load_au`, `chronic_load_au`, `acwr_status`; `safety_alert` is a separate boolean, not an `acwr_status` value). Would have thrown "record NEW has no field ..." the first time a row was ever inserted. Fixed to the real column names.
  8. The destructive `DROP PUBLICATION supabase_realtime CASCADE; CREATE PUBLICATION ... FOR TABLE (long hardcoded list)` block — the live publication currently contains only `tournament_day_plans` (confirmed via `pg_publication_tables`), so this would have silently deleted that from realtime; it also would have failed outright anyway since it listed `messages`/`performance_metrics`, neither of which exists. Removed entirely (nothing subscribes to Alert Engine realtime yet — alert-inbox polls over HTTP); left a comment with the safe additive `ALTER PUBLICATION ... ADD TABLE` for if it's wanted later.
  **Security hardening (found by the post-apply Supabase advisor run, per CLAUDE.md's "run get_advisors after DDL" practice):** the 6 new `SECURITY DEFINER` functions had mutable `search_path` (hijack risk) and were executable by `anon`/`authenticated` via direct RPC (letting any signed-in user forge an alert for an arbitrary `user_id`) — this project's default privileges grant new functions EXECUTE to `anon`/`authenticated` in addition to the `PUBLIC` default. Fixed in a follow-up migration (`20260723130000_harden_alert_engine_functions.sql`): pinned `search_path = public` on all 6, revoked EXECUTE from `PUBLIC`/`anon`/`authenticated` (kept for `service_role`; nothing legitimate calls these by name — they're invoked internally from the `trigger_*` functions, whose `SECURITY DEFINER` context isn't affected by the revoke). Re-ran the advisor after: 0 findings against any new table/function.
  **`alert-evaluate-rules.js` — every rule evaluator queried wrong table/column names.** `athlete_id` instead of `user_id`; `upper_bound_ratio`/`acute_load`/`chronic_load`/`cumulative_load_multiplier` instead of the real `personalized_safe_zone_max`/`acute_load_au`/`chronic_load_au`/`cumulative_multiplier`; a nonexistent `psychological_readiness_assessments` table (real name: `rtp_psychological_assessments`); `rtp_phase_progress.phase_advancement_ready`/`functional_criteria_blocked` (neither column exists — the real signal is `ready_for_next_phase`); an invalid PostgREST call comparing a column to a literal string expression (`.gte("acwr_ratio", "upper_bound_ratio * :threshold")`, the same invalid-syntax class as the `DISTINCT` bug found earlier this session); and a `check_underload_condition` RPC that was never defined anywhere. **Root cause of the redundancy:** 5 of these 6 rules already fire atomically via the DB triggers fixed above (insert/update on the source table), so re-implementing the same rule a second time in a polling job is itself a single-source-of-truth risk if the two ever diverge — rewritten to make those 5 explicit no-ops (with a comment pointing at the trigger that actually handles them) and only keep real query logic for Underload Alert, the one rule that genuinely needs a multi-day lookback no single-row trigger can express. Readiness Gate Failed and CMJ Depression Trend stay documented placeholders — neither has a queryable signal in the current schema.
  **`alert-resolve.js`** — `clearAutomationFlags` queried `acwr_snapshots` with `.eq("athlete_id", ...)` (real column: `user_id`) and tried to update a `load_restriction_active` column that doesn't exist. Silently swallowed (wrapped in try/catch, doesn't fail the request) so this degraded quietly rather than crashing — but the intended "clear the safety flag on resolve" behavior never actually ran. Fixed both.
  **A systemic, 16-file bug found by a test failure, not by inspection:** every one of these files calls `buildRequestLogContext(logger, event)` and treats the result as a logger with `.error()`/`.warn()` methods — but the real signature is `buildRequestLogContext(event, extraContext)`, returning a **plain data object**, not a logger. The correct pattern (used correctly elsewhere, e.g. `rtp-psychological-assessment.js`) is `logger.child(buildRequestLogContext(event))`. This means **every error-logging call in all 16 files threw `TypeError: requestLogger.error is not a function`** instead of returning a clean error response — a DB error, a validation failure, anything that hit a `requestLogger.error(...)`/`.warn(...)` call would crash with an unhandled exception. Found when a new test for `alert-evaluate-rules.js` exercised an error path and threw instead of asserting. Fixed identically across all 16: `alert-acknowledge.js`, `alert-dashboard.js`, `alert-evaluate-rules.js`, `alert-get-athlete.js`, `alert-preferences.js` (this session's own new file — inherited the bug by copying the existing pattern), `alert-resolve.js`, `injury-analytics.js`, `physio-protocol.js`, `recovery-effectiveness.js`, `rtp-advance-phase.js`, `rtp-all-protocols.js`, `rtp-create-assignment.js`, `rtp-protocol-assignment.js`, `rtp-record-assessment.js`, `rtp-team-protocols.js`, `team-acwr.js`.
  **`alert-preferences.js` (new) — built, and its own response-shape bug caught by its own test before shipping.** `AlertPreferencesComponent` had zero backend before this (task explicitly tracked as such). Built GET (defaults-merged-with-existing-rows for all 4 alert types) + PATCH (upsert on `(user_id, alert_type)`, `user_id` always forced from the authenticated caller, never trusted from the request body). First draft called `createSuccessResponse({success:true, data:merged})` — since `createSuccessResponse` already wraps its argument as `{success:true, data:<arg>}`, this double-nested to `{success:true, data:{success:true, data:merged}}`, and the component (raw `HttpClient`, no unwrapping helper) expects `response.data` to be the array directly. Caught by the GET test, fixed to `createSuccessResponse(merged)`.
  **`recovery-effectiveness.js` — genuinely GHOST since ship, three separate bugs deep, found while creating `athlete_recovery_logs`'s replacement.** (1) Queried/inserted `.from("recovery_logs")`, a table no migration had ever created — every call 500'd. Not part of the Alert Engine (the Phase 2a migration's own `athlete_recovery_logs`, dropped above, duplicated this same "athlete logs a recovery modality" concept with an incompatible, also-broken design) — this is a separate, standalone gap, fixed with its own small migration (`20260723120000_create_recovery_logs_table.sql`) matching exactly what the function already reads/writes. (2) The POST payload shape never matched the frontend: the backend expected `{modality_name, effectiveness_score, domain, logDate}`, but `recovery-effectiveness.component.ts` has always sent `{modality_name, log_date, effectiveness_1_10}` — even with the table fixed, every submission would 422 on `effectiveness_score` being undefined. Backend updated to accept the frontend's actual field names. (3) `tryParseJsonObjectBody` always returns `{ok, data, error}` — it never returns a falsy value or the parsed body directly — but the POST handler did `const payload = tryParseJsonObjectBody(event.body); if (!payload) {...}` and passed that `{ok,data,error}` object straight to `logRecoverySession` as if it were the parsed body. The `if (!payload)` check was dead code (always truthy), and every real field destructured to `undefined`, so **the log-a-session endpoint always 422'd regardless of what was sent** — independent of bugs (1) and (2). Fixed to check `parsedBody.ok` and pass `parsedBody.data`.
  **Verification:** all 3 migrations applied clean on final attempt; `list_tables` confirms 10 new tables live with correct RLS; security advisor 0 findings against anything new; `supabase-types.ts`/`live-schema.snapshot.json`/generated docs regenerated (201 tables, types 0 stale/0 live-missing); backend suite 1193 tests (+19 new: `alert-evaluate-rules`, `alert-preferences`, `alert-resolve`, `recovery-effectiveness` regression files), `lint:tooling`, `typecheck:functions` all green.

- **2026-07-23 — AUDIT (wider sweep): 5 more real bugs found and fixed across Angular routing/navigation and 2 RTP endpoints; Alert Engine (Phase 3) confirmed built-but-never-applied and flagged as a decision point, not silently resolved.** Continuation of the same-day RTP audit above, widened per an explicit "audit for pre/existing bugs, fix them" instruction — two parallel background agents (Angular, backend) surfaced candidates; each was verified directly (grep/read) before any fix, consistent with this file's standing practice.
  **Bug 1 — duplicate `"alerts"` route silently shadowed a whole screen.** `feature-routes.ts` registered `path: "alerts"` twice — once for the working `AlertsComponent` (reads `/api/coach-alerts`, the only screen a real nav link points to), once for `AlertInboxComponent` (Phase 3 Alert Engine). Angular's router matches array order, so the first entry always won and the second was permanently unreachable, independent of the Alert Engine's DB gap below. Fixed by renaming the shadowed route to `alert-inbox`.
  **Bug 2 — 14 onboarding `navigate()` calls targeted a route that doesn't exist.** All 7 staff onboarding components (`coach`/`head-coach`/`manager`/`nutritionist`/`physiotherapist`/`psychologist`/`strength-coach`) navigated to `/staff/dashboard` after completing or skipping — not a registered route, so it silently fell through to the top-level catch-all (`redirectTo: "today"`) and dumped staff users on the **athlete** home screen right after they finished (or skipped) their own professional profile. Fixed to navigate to `/staff/more` (the page they came from).
  **Bug 3 — 3 fully-built, documented-as-LIVE dashboard endpoints had no `netlify.toml` redirect.** `injury-analytics.js`, `team-acwr.js`, and `recovery-effectiveness.js` are complete, tested Netlify functions with matching Angular callers, and the §4 Ledger already listed their dashboards as LIVE — but none had a `[[redirects]]` block, so all three 404'd in production. This is likely the same gap the earlier 2026-07-22 entry flagged as "docs:regen's ORPHANED list contradicts the Ledger's LIVE claim" without resolving; this pass resolves it for these three (the ORPHANED-vs-Ledger discrepancy for the remaining `/api/rtp-*` names is explained by the two genuinely-broken functions fixed in the entry above, not a redirect gap — confirmed separately). Also fixed `recovery-effectiveness.component.ts`'s POST call, which targeted `/api/recovery-log` (wrong path, would have 404'd even after the redirect fix).
  **Bug 4 — invalid PostgREST syntax broke RTP phase advancement in two places.** Both `rtp-advance-phase.js` and `rtp-record-assessment.js` used `.select("DISTINCT criteria_id")` — not valid PostgREST syntax (no SQL `DISTINCT` in the select query param). In `rtp-advance-phase.js` this hard-500'd whenever the next phase had any real functional criteria defined — a physiotherapist could never advance an athlete past such a phase. In `rtp-record-assessment.js` the resulting error was silently swallowed (only `data`, not `error`, was destructured), so the `phaseAdvancementEligible` flag returned to the frontend was always `false` regardless of real progress. Fixed both by selecting plain `criteria_id` and deduping via `new Set(...).size` in JS (a criterion can have multiple `pass_fail=true` rows over time from retests, but should only count once); also fixed the swallowed error in `rtp-record-assessment.js` to be captured and logged. New regression tests: `rtp-advance-phase-validation.test.js` (5 tests), `rtp-record-assessment-validation.test.js` (8 tests) — both lock the dedup behavior and the previously-broken paths.
  **Flagged, not fixed — Alert Engine (Phase 3) is a product decision, not a bug fix.** `alert-inbox`/`alert-dashboard`/`alert-preferences` components and their backend functions are fully built, but the migrations that create their tables (`alert_rules`, `generated_alerts`, `alert_routes`, `alert_delivery_logs`, `alert_preferences`, `alert_subscriptions`, plus `acwr_snapshots`/`rtp_phase_progress`/`athlete_recovery_logs` from the companion Phase 2a migration) were never applied live — every screen in this feature will error until that's resolved. Applying them now is **not** a mechanical fix: (a) the Phase 2a migration's `psychological_assessments` table collides by name with an already-live table used by `staff-psychology.js`, with different columns — a plain `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` would silently no-op against it rather than create what the Alert Engine expects; (b) this touches ACWR/readiness alerting, which CLAUDE.md calls out for extra scrutiny before changing; (c) whether to ship a ~2-day-parked feature now, versus retire the dead routes, is a product call, not an engineering one. Per CLAUDE.md §3, this is flagged to the user with a proposed default (apply the migrations after resolving the naming collision) rather than decided silently.
  **Verification:** backend 1174 tests (+13 across the two new RTP test files), Angular 785 tests, `lint:tooling`, `typecheck:functions`, and Angular `tsc --noEmit` all green.

- **2026-07-23 — AUDIT: two Netlify functions completely broken since inception, found and fixed. One earlier doc claim in this same file corrected.** Following up on the "flagged, not fixed" `rtp-team-protocols.js` athlete_name note above led to a closer read of that file, which surfaced something much more severe.
  **Bug 1 (both `rtp-team-protocols.js` and `rtp-all-protocols.js`): every caller got 403, regardless of role, since inception.** Both files' inner handler passed to `baseHandler` was declared `async (event, _context) => {...}` — two parameters — then read `const userId = _context.user?.sub || _context.userId;`. `baseHandler` actually calls the inner handler with **three** arguments (`event, context, { userId, requestId, ... }`); the auth object is the third one. With only two parameters declared, `_context` receives the raw second positional argument (the original Netlify Lambda context, which has no `.user`/`.userId`), so `userId` was always `undefined` → `getUserRole(undefined)` always returns `null` → the role check always fails → **`GET /api/rtp/team/:teamId/protocols` and `GET /api/rtp/protocols` have 403'd for every single caller since these functions were written**, independent of the teamId bug below. Fixed by declaring the third parameter: `async (event, _context, { userId }) => {...}`, matching every other function in the codebase (a systematic check across all ~115 files calling `baseHandler` found no other instance of this pattern — these two were the only ones).
  **Bug 2 (`rtp-team-protocols.js` only): teamId was read from the wrong path segment.** Route is `/api/rtp/team/:teamId/protocols` — the code took the LAST path segment (`pathParts[pathParts.length - 1]`), which is always the literal string `"protocols"`, never the actual team ID (`"protocols"` trails `:teamId` in this route, unlike `rtp-protocol-assignment.js`'s `:athleteId/:injuryId` — no trailing segment — or `rtp-advance-phase.js`'s `:athleteId/:injuryId/phase`, which correctly accounts for the trailing `/phase` with a `-2`/`-3` offset). Fixed: `pathParts[pathParts.length - 2]`. Combined with Bug 1, this endpoint has never returned real data to a real caller.
  **Bug 3 (`rtp-all-protocols.js` only): the query embedded a table that doesn't exist.** `.select("... athletes!inner(id, user_id, first_name, last_name) ...")` — there is no `athletes` table live (confirmed against `live-schema.snapshot.json` and `supabase-types.ts`; `rtp_athlete_protocol_assignments.athlete_id`'s only FK targets `auth.users`, not any `public.athletes`). PostgREST fails the entire `select()` when any embedded relationship doesn't resolve, so even with Bug 1 fixed this endpoint would 500 on every call. Fixed by removing the embed and batch-fetching `users(id, first_name, last_name)` for the assignment's `athlete_id`s afterward — same pattern as the `rtp-team-protocols.js`/`physiotherapist-dashboard.js` fixes, just composing `first_name`+`last_name` (both NOT NULL columns) instead of using `full_name` (nullable), preserving this file's original intent.
  **Also fixed while there:** `rtp-team-protocols.js` never joined/returned `athlete_name` at all (the item this investigation started from) — same batched-lookup fix as `rtp-all-protocols.js`. Both files previously had zero test coverage; added `rtp-team-protocols-validation.test.js` (5 tests) and `rtp-all-protocols-validation.test.js` (4 tests), each covering the auth fix, the athlete-name join, and (for team-protocols) the teamId offset.
  **Doc correction (this file, same pass):** the TIER 1 Ledger row above and an earlier §6 entry claimed "a non-wildcard redirect can't carry the original path to `event.path`" as the reason `staff-profile.js` needed one function per role instead of one shared function with path-based dispatch. This investigation found strong contrary evidence: `alert-acknowledge.js`, `alert-resolve.js`, `rtp-protocol-assignment.js`, and `rtp-advance-phase.js` all correctly extract path segments/regex-match against `event.path` through redirects with named placeholders unreferenced in `to` (`:alertId`, `:athleteId`/`:injuryId`) — three to four independent implementations consistently relying on `event.path` preserving the original request path through exactly this redirect shape. The premise behind the 7-file split was very likely wrong. **This does not make the split a bug** — one function per role remains correct, tested, and unambiguous regardless of the mechanism — but the *documented reason* for it was probably incorrect, and is corrected here rather than left to mislead a future reader (the same standard this file holds every other entry to).
  **Verification:** backend 1161 tests (+9: 5 new `rtp-team-protocols` + 4 new `rtp-all-protocols`), `lint:tooling`, `typecheck:functions` all green.

- **2026-07-23 — SHIPPED: TIER 1 taken end-to-end LIVE (routes, admin review, document upload) — and a real duplication bug caught and retracted before shipping, not left live.** Continuation of the 2026-07-22 TIER 1 entries above, closing the 3 gaps that entry flagged.
  **Duplication caught and fixed (the significant one):** routing the components in required checking where each one's data actually lands, which surfaced that the athlete onboarding path duplicated existing, working functionality. `ProfileEditComponent` (`/profile/edit`) already edits position/height/weight/DOB via the canonical `POST /api/player-settings`, which writes **both** `users` (display) **and** `athlete_training_config` (the table the ACWR/readiness/age-recovery calculations actually read). The newly-built `AthleteOnboardingComponent` + `athlete-profile.js` wrote the same fields **only** to `users` — a second, divergent path that would have silently starved the engine's config table on any athlete who used the new screen instead of the old one. This is exactly the class of bug CLAUDE.md §4 exists to prevent, caught here before a single user saw it. **Fix, not a workaround:** deleted `AthleteOnboardingComponent`, `athlete-profile.js`, and its test outright (not deprecated, not left dead — removed); the athlete-specific NEW fields this session's migration `20260722b` actually added (`sport`/`years_experience`/`medical_history`/`emergency_contact_name`/`emergency_contact_phone` — none of which existed anywhere before) were instead threaded into the two *existing* canonical files: `player-settings.js` (write, mirrors onto `users` the same conditional `if (x !== undefined)` way height/weight already do) and `user-profile-core.js` (read, extends the same `SELECT`/response shape `ProfileEditComponent` already consumes). New coverage: `player-settings-athlete-fields.test.js` (2 tests asserting the mirror + that omitting the fields doesn't touch them) + 1 new case in the existing `user-profile-validation.test.js`. No new write path was left standing.
  **Routes wired (gap 1 closed):** all 7 role onboarding components now live at `/staff/profile/{role}`, `PhysiotherapistDashboardComponent` at `/staff/physiotherapist-dashboard`, new `CredentialReviewComponent` at `/staff/admin/credentials` — and, going further than "just routable" (the pre-existing `psychologist-dashboard`/`nutritionist-dashboard` routes are routed but linked from nowhere in the UI, a gap this doesn't fix but didn't repeat either): `staff-more.component` now shows a role-aware "Complete your professional profile" link (`staffProfileRouteFor(role)`, a pure function in `staff.guard.ts` — necessarily role-grained, not lane-grained, since `staffLaneFor` lumps head_coach/coach/manager/S&C into one "coach" lane but each needs a different form), a physio-only injury-roster link, and an admin-only credential-review link.
  **Admin credential review built (gap 2 closed):** new `admin-credentials.js` — GET lists `credential_verifications` (optionally `?status=`) joined with the submitter's name/role (batched, no N+1); POST `{id, action: 'verify'|'reject', reason?}` updates status and, on verify, flips `credentials_verified=true` on the matching profile table (role → table map, mirroring `staff-profile.js`'s own route config so the two stay consistent). **Deliberately avoids path-segment routing entirely** (id/action travel via query-string/body, not a URL path segment) — a second, independent way of sidestepping the same event.path ambiguity the 2026-07-22 entry hit with the profile endpoints, this time by design rather than by a caught mistake. New `CredentialReviewComponent`: tabs by status, approve/reject with a reason field, "View document" opens a 10-minute signed URL (never a public one — the bucket is private). Owner/admin gated both server-side (`getUserRole`) and in the UI (`isAdmin` computed in `staff-more`). 8 new integration tests.
  **Document upload wired (gap 3 closed, for one role):** new private Storage bucket `credential-documents` (migration `20260722c`, applied live — 10MB cap, `application/pdf`/`image/jpeg`/`image/png` only; RLS: a user reads/writes only their own `{userId}/...`-prefixed folder, an owner/admin can read any file in the bucket). `staff-profile.js`'s credential-filing step now uploads a provided base64 document to this bucket and stores the resulting **path** (not a public URL) as `credential_verifications.document_url`, skipping the upload entirely when the credential already exists (matches the existing idempotent-filing behavior — no orphaned re-uploads on a re-save). `physiotherapist-onboarding.component.ts`'s file picker — previously logged the filename and did nothing else — now actually reads the file via `FileReader`, validates type/size client-side too, and includes it in the submit payload. New coverage: 3 tests (successful upload + path stored, disallowed mime type rejected without failing the save, no re-upload on an existing credential). **Honest scope limit:** the other 6 onboarding forms have no file-picker UI at all yet (only physiotherapist's does) — the backend accepts a document for any role via the same payload fields, so adding the picker to the remaining 6 forms is pure frontend work, not a backend gap. Since a credential can be filed and reviewed by license/certification number alone, this doesn't block the verification workflow end-to-end, just means most roles can't yet attach a scan.
  **Also fixed in the same pass:** `docs-regen.mjs`'s `STORAGE_BUCKETS` allowlist (used to suppress false "unknown table ref" warnings for `.storage.from(bucket)` calls, which the scanner's regex can't distinguish from `.from(table)`) didn't include the new `credential-documents` bucket — added, same fix class as the pre-existing `community-media`/`avatars`/`team-logos`/`database-backups` entries.
  **Verification:** backend 1152 tests (+9 net over the 2026-07-22 entry: -5 deleted athlete-profile tests, +2 player-settings mirror, +1 user-profile field, +3 net staff-profile document-upload, +8 admin-credentials) / Angular 785 tests / `lint:tooling` / `eslint "src/app/**/*.{ts,html}"` (0 errors) / `typecheck:functions` / `tsc --noEmit` (angular) all green. `ng build` could not be run in this sandbox (Node version below the Angular CLI's minimum — pre-existing environment limitation, not attempted to work around); `tsc --noEmit` is the actual CI gate and is clean.

- **2026-07-22 — SHIPPED: TIER 1 backend wired live (9 new Netlify functions), 2 schema follow-ups applied, and a genuine CI-blocking TypeScript regression fixed in the same pass.** Continuation of the same day's TIER 1 migration entry above.
  **Schema follow-ups (applied live via Supabase MCP, local migration files added for reproducibility):** building the profile-CRUD endpoints surfaced that two onboarding forms (built earlier the same day) collect fields the original TIER 1 migration never added columns for. `20260722a_extend_coach_profiles_head_coach_fields.sql` adds `years_as_head_coach`/`coaching_background`/`team_development_approach` to `coach_profiles`; `20260722b_extend_users_athlete_profile_fields.sql` adds `sport`/`years_experience`/`medical_history`/`emergency_contact_name`/`emergency_contact_phone` to `users` (the athlete form's data — reused the existing canonical bio-profile table rather than creating a new one; `youth_athlete_settings` was considered and rejected as a fit since it's explicitly youth/parental-consent scoped, not a general adult athlete profile). Both are additive-only, 0 new security-advisor findings, types regenerated (0 stale), snapshot rebuilt (table-level set still matches live at 191 — column-only changes). Named `20260722a`/`20260722b` (not a `HHMMSS` suffix) because a plain numeric timestamp prefix always sorts *before* `20260722_staff_profiles_and_credentials.sql` lexicographically (a digit is less than `_`) — which would replay the ALTER TABLEs before the CREATE TABLE they depend on in a fresh `supabase db reset`. Verified the letter-suffixed names sort correctly after it.
  **Backend (9 new functions + `staff-profile.js` shared module, 16 new integration tests, all green):** `staff-{physiotherapist,nutritionist,psychologist,strength-coach,coach,head-coach,manager}-profile.js`, `athlete-profile.js`, `physiotherapist-dashboard.js`. **A real design bug was caught before it shipped:** the first draft used one shared `staff-profile.js` function with `event.path`-based routing across all 7 role redirects — but a non-wildcard `[[redirects]]` entry (`from = "/api/staff/x-profile"` with no `/*`) doesn't carry the original request path through to the function's `event.path`, so a single function has no way to tell which of the 7 routes it was reached through. Caught by working out the actual Netlify request-forwarding contract before writing tests, not by a failing test. Fixed by converting `staff-profile.js` into a shared route-factory module (`createProfileHandler(routeKey)`, no `handler` export of its own — so Netlify doesn't try to deploy it directly) plus one 2-line wrapper file per role, matching this repo's pre-existing `staff-nutritionist.js`/`staff-physiotherapist.js`/`staff-psychology.js` convention. `physiotherapist-dashboard.js` and `athlete-profile.js` were never affected (single-route functions that don't need path-based dispatch). Role-gated server-side via `team_members.role` (not just row-level self-service RLS) so a player can't POST to `/api/staff/physiotherapist-profile` and self-declare as a verified clinician — RLS alone would have allowed that (it only checks `auth.uid() = user_id`, not what role the caller actually holds). Credential filing (self-reported → `credential_verifications`, `status='pending'`) is idempotent per (user_id, credential_type, credential_name) so re-saving the onboarding form doesn't spam duplicate review rows.
  **A pre-existing, unrelated, CI-blocking regression was found and fixed in the same pass:** `npx tsc --noEmit` in `angular/` (the exact command `ci.yml` runs) was RED — 4 errors, all in the `return-to-play/` RTP module the 2026-07-21 entry above claims was already fixed clean. Root cause was type-shape duplication: `physio-protocol-dashboard.component.ts` hand-declares its own local `ProtocolAssignment` interface instead of importing the one `rtp.service.ts` already exports, and it had drifted narrower than reality on two fields — `estimated_return_date: string` (DB column is nullable; the service's own type already said `string | null`; the component's OWN code at line 598 already defensively guarded `if (!a?.estimated_return_date) return 0` before using it, proof this was a latent bug, not a behavior change) and `individual_modifiers: Record<string, string | number | boolean>` (service type: `Record<string, unknown> | null`). Separately, `rtp.service.ts`'s `AdvancePhaseResponse.assignment` was typed as non-nullable `ProtocolAssignment` while its own fallback literal on a failed/empty API response sets `assignment: null` — copy-paste miss from `ProtocolAssignmentResponse` one interface above it, which already has this correctly as `ProtocolAssignment | null`. `rtp-assessment-modal.component.ts` had a direct `payload as Record<string, unknown>` cast TS rejects because `AssessmentPayload` doesn't structurally overlap with an index signature; fixed via `payload as unknown as Record<string, unknown>`, exactly what the compiler's own error message suggests. All three fixes are type-only (widen an over-narrow local type to match what the data actually is, or thread a cast through `unknown`) — zero runtime behavior change, verified by re-running the full Angular suite (785 tests, 0 failures) after. **Not fixed (flagged only):** the component's local `ProtocolAssignment` duplicating the service's exported type at all is the root cause that let this drift happen once already; importing the shared type instead of redeclaring it would close the class of bug permanently, but is a larger, more invasive change than this pass's scope — left as a follow-up. **Verification: backend 1143 tests / Angular 785 tests / `lint:tooling` / `eslint "src/app/**/*.{ts,html}"` / `typecheck:functions` / `tsc --noEmit` (angular) all green before push.**

Security advisor re-run post-migration: 0 findings against the 7 new tables. `supabase-types.ts` regenerated (0 stale, 0 live-missing). 8 Angular onboarding components + `PhysiotherapistDashboardComponent` were built and committed (`d70acb9`) but **the backend endpoints they call do not exist yet** — see the §4 Ledger row (PARTIAL, not LIVE) for the full gap list. **Drift 1 — FIXED: `live-schema.snapshot.json` had gone stale independent of this migration.** Regenerating it surfaced 6 tables live since earlier this session (Phase 1D's `rtp_athlete_protocol_assignments`/`rtp_criteria_assessments`/`rtp_functional_criteria`/`rtp_protocol_definitions`/`rtp_protocol_phases`, plus `zz_video_backup_20260719`) that were never captured — the snapshot's last refresh (2026-07-18) predates them, and nobody re-ran the refresh when those migrations landed (a §2 miss). Rebuilt the snapshot from full live introspection rather than patch the delta, so `generatedOn`/table count/list are all directly verified against live, not incremented by assumption — now 191 tables, matching live exactly (confirmed via `list_tables` diff, zero remaining mismatch either direction). **Drift 2 — FIXED: this file's header/§2/§3 rolled-up counts (178 tables/121 functions/265 migrations, dated 2026-07-19) were stale relative to live** (191/139/276) — the gap predates this session's migration and traces to the 2026-07-21 Phase 1D/1E/2d work (RTP tables + 6 staff dashboards, per the existing dated Ledger rows) shipping without the header being rolled forward, itself a §8 violation at the time. Corrected header/§2/§3 to measured-live values. **Flagged, not resolved:** `docs:regen`'s ORPHANED list for `/api/rtp-*`, `injury-analytics`, `physio-protocol`, `recovery-effectiveness`, `recovery-recommendations`, `team-acwr` contradicts the 2026-07-21 Ledger rows' claim that those dashboards are LIVE and wired — either the static orphan-scanner is missing a dynamic call site, or those rows overstated wiring. Needs a direct grep pass to resolve; not attempted here (out of scope for the migration work this entry documents — the discrepancy predates this session and isn't something this migration touched). **Pre-existing, unrelated, surfaced by the same advisor run:** `public.zz_video_backup_20260719` has RLS disabled (ERROR-level finding) — a manual backup table with no migration file (hence its `DATA_MODEL.md` DRIFT flag). Not auto-remediated (enabling RLS with zero policies would just lock it) — flagged to the owner; remediation SQL is `ALTER TABLE public.zz_video_backup_20260719 ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;` plus whatever access policies are actually needed, an owner call on what those should be.

- **2026-07-19 — SHIPPED: competition "how it went" load model (one/both-ways × players-on-day × surface now scale the ACWR feed), game formats corrected to 2×12/2×15/2×20, and a real `totalMinutes` 0-100 cap bug fixed.** **(a) Formats:** the post-event card offered 2×12/2×20/**2×40**; 2×40 does not exist in flag — corrected to the three sanctioned formats **2×12 / 2×15 / 2×20** (IFAF/AFFL play 2×20; domestic runs 2×12 and 2×15). Default stays 2×20 (40 min). **(b) New load model — single source `angular/src/app/competition/competition-load.util.ts` (CLAUDE.md §4):** `effectiveGameMinutes = games × minutesPerGame × wayFactor × benchFactor × surfaceFactor`, folded into the participation `totalMinutes` that the Foster sRPE pipeline (`record_event_participation` → competition `training_sessions`, workload = minutes × RPE) turns into ACWR load. **Owner-set factors (2026-07-19, tunable in that one file):** both-ways carries **1.5×** one-way (baseline both-ways = 1.0 = the app's prior flat full-minutes behaviour; one-way = 1/1.5 ≈ 0.667 — so this only ever LOWERS a one-way player below what shipped before, the safe direction); short bench **+0.10 per player below a 7-deep rotation, cap +0.30** (a bare 5-player squad plays everything); artificial turf **+15%** vs natural grass. Unknown players/surface are neutral (× 1.0, never inferred — Law #7). Numbers locked by `competition-load.util.spec.ts` (7 cases) and independently verified against real transpiled execution (14/14). **(c) Surface-vs-dose — deliberate, and distinct from the 2026-07-18 decision:** that ledger row keeps surface "advisory-only, no ENGINE input, no dose reads it" — and that stays true for the periodization engine's SCHEDULED-event inputs (surface still doesn't move a prescription). This change is a different pathway: the athlete's own POST-EVENT participation actual, where the owner chose (2026-07-19) to let the surface they actually played on scale that one logged session's load. The engine remains surface-blind; only a hand-logged competition actual now reflects turf. **(d) Real bug fixed in the same pass:** `event-participation.js` validated `totalMinutes` with the games cap (`parseGamesInt`, 0-100), so any day over ~2 games of 2×20 (8 games × 40 = 320) silently **422'd** — multi-game participation logging was broken. New `parseMinutesInt` caps at 600 (matches `weekend-games`' "a full tournament day"). Regression-tested (320 → 201, 601 → 422). **(e) Semantics (flagged):** the multiplier is folded into `totalMinutes` because `record_event_participation` is a fixed RPC scoring load as minutes × RPE — so the stored competition `duration_minutes` is now **load-equivalent** minutes, not wall-clock. A migration-adding `p_load_multiplier` (or a stored effective-AU) would keep clock minutes separate; deferred as an owner call. The one/both-ways · players · surface breakdown is recorded in the session `notes` for coach transparency so the heavier number isn't unexplained. Backend suites green (`event-participation-validation` 13, `calibration-logs-validation` unaffected). **NOT run in this environment:** the Angular unit run / `tsc` / `ng build` (the component + template wiring) — the ng toolchain wasn't installed in the sandbox this shipped from; CI (Angular vitest + strictTemplates build) is the gate for the frontend half.
