(b) **This entry** — the 2026-07-19 `acwr-band` swap (commit `e9267852`) deleted `shared/acwr-trend.component.ts` and `shared/perf-viz/load-heatmap.component.ts` and wired the richer banded ACWR onto Stats + `/acwr`; it shipped without a §6 note (a §8 violation on my part), so the pre-existing 2026-07-09/-10 entries below still describe `app-acwr-trend` as live. - **2026-07-19 — Knowledge-base + evidence-basis audit: content verified HIGH quality and accurate; the only real gap is structural (citation LINKAGE), not correctness. No content was rewritten (it doesn't need it, and clinical content is owner-gated — §3).** 122 KB entries, all Merlin-approved and evidence-graded. **Spot-checked the highest-harm entries (those with dosage_guidelines) and every checkable number matches the position stands verified elsewhere this session:** caffeine 3–6 mg/kg / 45–60 min pre / ~5 h half-life / 6 h sleep gap (ISSN, Guest 2021); beta-alanine 3.2–6.4 g/day divided for 60–240 s efforts (ISSN); collagen ~15 g + vit-C ~50 mg 30–60 min pre-load; injury protein 2.0–2.5 g/kg/day; NMT ACL block (hop-and-stick/Copenhagen/Nordic, ~10 min 3–4×/wk). The `acwr_evidence_honest_status` entry is exemplary — it cites the null RCT (Dalen-Lorentsen 2021, 482 athletes) and refuses the causal claim, matching the engine's own walk-back. **Citations are REAL, not hallucinated:** verified PMID 42043072 against PubMed — the entry's claim ("108 national-team players, 34 F/74 M, 62% injured, hip circ 103 cm → 5× ACL", Vázquez-Villarreal) matches the abstract exactly; the study is flag-football-specific and recent (2026). **The one real gap is STRUCTURAL:** entries cite PMIDs *inline in prose* but the structured `supporting_articles` array is empty on all dosage entries (only **18/122** entries link articles), and the `research_articles` table holds just **3 rows** (last touched 2026-02) — it's an underused structured store, not stale knowledge (the KB content itself cites 2026 studies). So "update the KB" is **not** a content task — the content is current and correct — it's an optional data-hygiene migration: extract the PMIDs already present in the prose into `supporting_articles`/`research_articles` for machine-traceable evidence links. **Proposed, not done** (touches 122 live athlete-facing rows for a traceability gain, not a correctness fix — owner's call on priority). Full 122-entry clinical re-verification + any new-content authoring remain owner-gated efforts, deliberately not attempted in one pass.

- **2026-07-19 — RPC-signature + write-payload audit across ALL functions (the other ~114 endpoints). One latent bug fixed; 6 ghost RPCs + 1 dead AI route found and classified, not blind-fixed.** Method: extracted every `.rpc("name", {params})` on both sides (52 calls) and diffed the param sets against the LIVE `pg_proc` signatures; separately cross-checked client write payloads against server parsers. **FIXED (real, low-risk): `import-process.js` called `log_training_session` with `p_session_load` (the arg is `p_workload`) and `p_source` (no such arg).** PostgREST resolves a function by its named-arg set, so every import row 404'd — the CSV/manual import path was fully broken. Currently UNREACHABLE (no Angular caller of `/api/import/process`), so it was a latent landmine rather than a live break, and the sibling `import-open-data.js` already used `p_workload` correctly; aligned it. **6 GHOST RPCs (called in code, exist in NO schema) — classified, NOT auto-fixed because the fix is "create the function" or "delete the feature", a backend/product call:** `calculate_acwr` (client `wellness.service.ts#calculateAcwr` — **dead: zero component callers**, so harmless); `compute_coach_analytics` (`coach-analytics.js`, coach lane — reachability uncertain, would error if the analytics endpoint is hit); `generate_proactive_checkins` / `get_relevant_conversation_context` / `learn_user_preferences` / `search_knowledge_semantic` (all in `utils/smart-ai-service.js` — an experimental AI-personalization layer; the RPCs don't exist, so those augmentation paths either never run or throw-and-swallow, never corrupting core data). **1 DEAD ROUTE:** `/api/ai/process-command` — the client method `ai.service#processNaturalCommand` has zero callers and `ai-chat.js` reads no `command` field; isolated dead code. **Verified sound, no change:** `award_achievement`'s apparent extra params (`streak`/`score`/…) were a scanner false positive — they're nested inside `p_context: JSON.stringify({…})`, not top-level RPC args (a brace-extractor flattening nested keys — a third audit-method trap now recorded). `upsert_wellness_checkin`, `complete_training_session`, `log_training_session` (the correct caller), `update_player_streak`, `record_event_participation`, the account-lifecycle + community-counter RPCs all match live signatures exactly. Ghost-*table* refs independently re-confirmed 0 (`docs:regen`). **Recommendation for the owner:** the 6 ghost RPCs are the real open item — each is either (a) create the missing Postgres function, or (b) remove the dead call. `calculate_acwr` (dead client method) and `process-command` (dead route) are safe deletions; the coach-analytics + smart-ai ones need a "is this feature meant to exist?" decision before either building the RPC or deleting the caller.

- **2026-07-19 — Legacy + contract audit (onboarding → whole write flow): one real data-loss break found and fixed; several suspected breaks disproven by testing against production.** **Legacy:** effectively none left — 0 constructor-param DI, 0 `@Input`/`@Output` decorators, 0 `ngClass`/`ngStyle`, 0 `toPromise`, 0 async-pipe; one trivial `@HostListener` (Escape, training screen) noted, not worth churn. **Contracts, verified field-by-field on both sides:** `team-join` ✓; onboarding→`player-settings` ✓ via its alias layer (whose own comment records this exact mismatch as a past shipped bug) — with **one REAL break: `preferredTime`**. Onboarding asked Morning/Afternoon/Evening and sent the answer under a key no server code has ever read — the athlete's answer was silently discarded, and no such column exists anywhere. **Fixed (owner call 2026-07-19): the question is now a real HH:MM time input saved through the same `teamTrainingDays.time` channel Settings uses**, which `periodization-prescription` reads independently of `days` (verified) — so the Phase 5b cooler-hour anchor now exists from day one instead of only after a Settings visit. Wellness check-in ✓ **verified against the live RPC signature** (all 14 `p_*` params of `upsert_wellness_checkin` match; the component→service name mapping `sleep→p_sleep_quality` etc. is the real contract — the client calls the RPC directly, not the netlify function). Body check→`athlete-injuries` ✓; training log ✓ (server aliases `date`→`sessionDate`, `type`→`sessionType`); hydration ✓ (0–5000 ml validated). **Disproven by testing prod, not assumed:** bare `/api/weather` (the one live-called path with a splat-only redirect) looked like the team-join SPA-fallthrough bug class — `curl` against production returned proper function JSON, proving Netlify's `/*` splat matches the bare path; the weather guard is fine and NO redirect change was made. **Removed:** 3 `API_ENDPOINTS` families whose endpoints do not exist server-side (`fixtures` — table dropped in `20260530`; `tournaments.*`; `player.notifyInactive`) — any future caller would have received the SPA's HTML; and `scripts/audit-routes.js` + its npm script — a stale tool checking an `entry:` route convention deleted in the static-first rebuild (`de726eef`), failing 100% of routes by design drift, wired into no CI. **Known, flagged, not actioned:** (a) `tests/contracts/run-contracts-test.js` requires service-role creds and is wired into NO CI job — the db-integration job has the secrets, but wiring an unexercised test in blind could redden main; decide deliberately. (b) `API_ENDPOINTS` is a bypassed catalogue — 37 of 45 groups have zero references (screens call path literals), so the map is drift-prone documentation; the 3 provably-dead families are gone, the rest point at real endpoints and were left. (c) Two audit-method traps recorded for the next auditor: a `payload.X` grep misses destructuring (`const { weightKg } = payload` — jersey/height/weight looked dropped but are mirrored onto `users`), and a redirect-coverage checker must exclude the `/*`→`index.html` SPA fallback or it "covers" everything (mine did, until a planted-positive self-test caught it).

- **2026-07-19 — 4 TEST EVENTS REMOVED from live, and two owner decisions recorded.** (a) **Test events deleted — they were distorting the engine, not just cluttering it.** Investigating why the new surface advisory fired for nobody surfaced 4 future competition events (Elite Bowl 2026 / Vienna / 4 games / **importance=peak**, and Autumn Flag League GD1–GD3 / 3 games each). They were created **2026-07-10 by `aljkous@gmail.com` through the coach event form** while the events feature was being built — every other event in the table is `created_by IS NULL` and predates them by two months. The owner did not recognise Elite Bowl, all four had **zero** attached activity (no `event_games`, no `event_availability`, no `event_participation`), and the standing directive is "never use test or mock data, only the real data". **Why this was not cosmetic:** Elite Bowl was the athlete's `nextEvent` at `importance='peak'`, and peak events drive the deepest taper + post-event recovery — from late August it would have shaped real prescriptions toward a competition that does not exist. (PAST fake events are worse in a different way: `estimateGameLoads` folds them into ACWR chronic load — CALCULATIONS §1.) Deleted along with their 2 now-orphaned `competitions` rows; **restore script committed at `docs/ground-truth/2026-07-19-removed-test-events-restore.sql`** (IDs preserved) in case any turns out to be a genuine fixture. Live is now back to **0 future events, 9 past** — which restores the state the live-accounts note describes. **Correction to the entry this replaces:** it claimed GD1/GD3 were set to `surface='turf'` and the advisory was "now live for real events". That was written before the rows were known to be test data — the turf setting went with them, and **the surface advisory currently fires for nobody again**, correctly, because no real event has a surface yet. It activates when a coach sets one on a genuine fixture. **RESOLVED — the other 9 past events are REAL and must stay.** They looked like seed data on every DB signal (`created_by IS NULL`, one batch on 2026-05-08, nothing in the schema marks provenance — `v_seed_integrity` is a referential-integrity view, not a seed marker). The repo settled it: `database/seeds/sample_2026_spring_schedule.sql`, added in `c51b7b97` on **2026-05-08** — the same date — whose own header reads *"a sample seed for dev / staging that loads **one athlete's real 2026 spring schedule**"*. The MECHANISM was a seed; the CONTENT is Aljosa's genuine spring season: Slovenian Cup, SVN League R1–R2, AT League R1–R3 + Finals, Copenhagen Bowl (8 games) and Big Bowl (8 games), 2026-05-09 → 2026-06-21, all International Frogs. The seed writes to **only** `competitions` + `competition_events` — it fabricates no training sessions, RSVPs, participation or wellness, so no performance data was ever invented. **Deleting them would have been the harmful move:** past events feed `estimateGameLoads` → ACWR chronic load (CALCULATIONS §1), so removing a real season deflates chronic load, makes the athlete read as less conditioned than they are, and pushes the engine toward `building_base` / under-prescription. "Named like a fixture" is not the same as "is a fixture" — the file name says `sample`, the data is real. **`status='scheduled'` on past events is NOT a gap — checked, and this entry previously overstated it.** All 9 still read `scheduled`, and nothing transitions events to `completed`. That is fine: `competition_events.status` is only ever compared against `'cancelled'` (four `status !== 'cancelled'` filters in `schedule.js`; nothing anywhere distinguishes `scheduled` from `completed`), and event status is **never rendered in any template**. It is carried on the client model as `EventStatus` and never read. So a past event marked "scheduled" has zero behavioural and zero user-visible effect — mutating 9 live rows to fix something invisible would be risk without benefit. If a future feature ever needs "did this happen", it should key off the date or introduce a real completion signal, not retrofit meaning onto a field the code treats as a cancelled-flag. **Process smell worth knowing:** a file that explicitly says "NOT a deployed migration … for dev / staging" was run against LIVE. The data being real makes it harmless here; the next such file might not be. (b) **Fail-open confirmed as intended, for now.** If the injury or travel fetch fails, the injury guard and arrival-day cap simply don't fire — an athlete could be handed a full session. This predates the `resource()` work and was preserved through it; owner's call 2026-07-19 is **leave as-is and revisit**. It is no longer implicit: both services carry a SAFETY NOTE and both behaviours are pinned by tests (`injury.service.spec.ts`, `event-travel.service.spec.ts`), so a future change is a deliberate one rather than an accident. Revisiting it means deciding whether "restrictions unknown" should hold back high-intensity work rather than proceed — an engine behaviour change, scoped separately.

- **2026-07-18 — Angular 22 modernization sweep: 6 services to `resource()`, first Signal Forms migration, a new CI guard — and one self-inflicted safety regression, found and fixed before it shipped.** Context: an audit found the app already fully standalone / 100% OnPush / zoneless / signal-inputs, so the real legacy pockets were narrower than expected — RxJS-heavy data fetching (41 `.subscribe` sites vs **one** `resource()`) and hand-rolled forms. **Migrated to `resource()`:** `qb-throwing` (pilot), `event-games`, `athlete-events`, `event-travel`, `injury`, plus a fix to the pre-existing `schedule`. Convention written down in `angular/src/app/core/services/README.md`. **Deliberately NOT migrated, with reasons recorded:** `protocol` (a command, and its consumer's `protocolTriggered` latch is load-bearing — ticking an exercise is component-local state keyed by POSITIONAL index, so a mid-session re-realize would corrupt an athlete's progress), `readiness` (an event-triggered command carrying a monotonic out-of-order guard on a safety-critical score), `acwr` + `channel` (realtime PUSH services; `resource()` is a pull primitive). **Two real bugs found in existing code:** (1) `schedule.service.ts` read `computed(() => resource.value() ?? null)` — and `resource.value()` THROWS in an error state, so a transient `/api/schedule` failure threw through `snapshot()` → `nextEvent`/`currentPhase`/`density*` into ~10 consumers including the periodization inputs, instead of degrading to an empty schedule (fixed `7938d5d8`, regression-specced); (2) `injury.service` state was being cleared from outside by `periodization.service` (`injury.active.set([])` on logout) to prevent a second athlete on the same device inheriting the first's injury — keying the resource on `userId` made that write unnecessary rather than impossible, so the leak is now structurally closed instead of hand-guarded. **The self-inflicted one:** the migration pattern `computed(() => res.hasValue() ? res.value() : [])` looked right and was wrong, because Angular's `resource()` DISCARDS its previous value when a **reload** fails. The pre-resource services only ever assigned on success, so a failed refetch left good data standing; the naive pattern meant one flaky refetch cleared it. On two safety paths that mattered — `injury` (restrictions vanish ⇒ the injury guard stops firing ⇒ an injured athlete can be prescribed a full session) and `event-travel` (arrival hours vanish ⇒ no arrival-day cap after a flight). It was committed across three migrations before being caught by asking, late, what a failed reload actually does. Fixed by `core/services/resource-last-good.ts` (`lastGoodByKey`), which keeps the last successfully-loaded value across a failed reload but DROPS it the instant the key changes — because "keep last good" alone reintroduces the phantom-injury leak. Its first implementation used `linkedSignal`'s `previous`, passed a hand-driven probe, and failed its own spec: signals are lazy, so a resolved→error transition nobody observed never captured the value; it uses an `effect` now. Afterwards, all five migrations were re-audited systematically (public surface / initial-load failure / reload failure / error semantics / loading semantics / request counts) — no further divergences; 4 of the 5 `error` signals turn out to have no consumer at all. **Also:** first Signal Forms migration (`device-data` + `core/forms/device-session.schema.ts`) which moved the "never write an all-null objective-load row" rule (Law #7) from a `canSave` computed behind a disabled button into a declared validator testable without TestBed; that plus dropping two dead `FormsModule` imports removes **all** legacy `@angular/forms` usage from the app. **New CI guard:** `npm run check:signals` (`scripts/check-signal-mutation.mjs`) — under OnPush + zoneless, mutating a signal's value in place re-renders nothing (silent staleness). The audit found the codebase already clean (0 violations, 184 files), so the script exists to keep it that way; it self-tests against 5 planted positives and 5 negatives before reporting, because an earlier scan in the same session reported "clean" only because its regex was broken.

- **2026-07-18 — SHIPPED: playing-surface awareness + two game-day advisories (surface, cramping); 2 doc drifts fixed in the same pass.** Closes the surface gap the nutrition/surface research flagged: the app previously had **zero** surface awareness (the only "turf" strings in the repo were exercise descriptions). **Schema:** migration `20260718120000_add_surface_to_events` adds a nullable `surface` (`CHECK IN ('grass','turf')`) to **both** `competition_events` and `athlete_events` and appends `ce.surface` to `v_athlete_schedule` (append-only `CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW`, every existing column preserved in order). Applied live via Supabase MCP; snapshot + `supabase-types.ts` + `docs:regen` refreshed in the same commit (`docs:regen`: 178 tables, **0 DRIFT**, 0 stale-in-types; the types diff was exactly the 7 expected `surface` lines and nothing else, which also re-confirms the types file had no pre-existing drift). **Thread-through** mirrors `venue` exactly — the canonical `PlayingSurface` type lives in `schedule.models.ts` (the spine, per its own "add new fields here" rule) and is re-exported by `athlete-event.models.ts`; both event write paths validate it (`athlete-events.js` uses the nullable-enum `tier` pattern), both entry forms expose a chiprow (athlete schedule form + coach `staff/events`), and NULL/unknown is the default. **Nothing infers a surface, and no engine input, dose, prescription or macro target reads it** — this is deliberately additive/display-only, matching the modest strength of the evidence. **Two advisory modules** (`gameday/surface-advisory.ts`, `gameday/cramp-guidance.ts` — pure, TestBed-free, 35 tests, following the `today/unlogged-practice.ts` pattern), plus a new optional `gameDayEnvironment` evidence-preset section + `getGameDayEnvironmentEvidence()`, modelled on `injuryPrevention` but deliberately **not** as "modifiableRiskFactors" (you cannot train your way out of the venue's surface — these shift ADVICE, never dose). Four PubMed-verified citations added: `gould2022` (10.1177/03635465211069562), `venishetty2024` (10.1177/23259671241265378), `schwellnus2008` (10.1136/bjsm.2008.050401), `nelson2016` (10.1002/mus.25176). **Evidence correction caught during the build:** an earlier framing carried in-session cited "Gould 2022 / Ranson 2018" for "similar overall risk, higher foot/ankle". Re-pulling from PubMed confirmed Gould is real and says that — but also carries a **football-specific** finding that framing missed ("football players, particularly those at high levels, were more likely to sustain a knee injury on artificial turf"), which is now the actual stated reason the knee/patellar tendon sit in the advisory's sensitive-region set rather than the weaker "same landing load" analogy that was written first. The dissenting NFL cohort (Venishetty 2024: higher overall LE rate 1.42 vs 1.22/game, season-ending-surgery OR 1.60) is carried alongside so the copy stays inside a genuinely unsettled picture; the un-verifiable "Ranson 2018" reference was dropped rather than shipped. **Real bug avoided:** the surface advisory reads `InjuryService.active()`, **not** `restrictions()`. `deriveRestrictions` returns `null` unless an injury carries a sprint/throwing-restricting `restrictionType`, and only collects regions from those — so keying on it would have made the feature **silently never fire** for the still-playing niggle (inflamed Achilles, runner's knee) that is precisely its target case. **Doc drift 1 — FIXED:** §3 said "121 functions (117 exercised, 4 orphaned … `auth-reset-password`)" while this file's own header said 118/3 — an internal contradiction dating from the 2026-07-17 entry below, which corrected the header but not §3. `auth-reset-password.js` was deleted; the generated ENDPOINTS.md (118/3) is authoritative. **Doc drift 2 — FIXED:** the V2.4 tier Ledger row claimed "No UI creates team-scheduled `competitions`/`competition_events` at all (zero write endpoints) — team-side tiers are engine-ready but DB-only to set." Both halves are wrong: `staff/events/events.component.ts` + `CompetitionEventsService` find-or-create `competitions` and insert `competition_events` (and update format + hotel), and the create form has a full **Level** chiprow (club → olympic) wired to `competitions.level`. These are direct RLS-guarded Supabase client writes, not Netlify functions — which is why the original endpoint-shaped grep missed them, and the lesson is logged here rather than only fixed. Verification: backend 1064 / Angular 638 / all 4 typechecks / eslint + ng lint / lint:tooling + tokens + design-system / check:consent / prettier / production `ng build` all green before push.

- **2026-07-18 — EVIDENCE AUDIT (calculations ↔ primary literature). Verified correct + 2 drifts fixed.** Matched every load-bearing calculation carrying an evidence claim against the primary sources (PubMed). **Verified sound, no change needed:** caffeine dosing (`CAFFEINE` 3–6 mg/kg, ~60 min pre — matches the ISSN caffeine position stand, Guest 2021, doi:10.1186/s12970-020-00383-4 exactly); WBGT heat guard (Australian BoM approximation formula transcribed correctly; NATA/ACSM continuous-activity flag thresholds 25.7/27.8/30/32.2 °C correct; honest null-humidity fallback); ACWR *module* (`acwr.js` — already walked back the false-precision risk multipliers, cites the Dalen-Lorentsen 2021 null cluster-RCT, keeps advisory bands only — exemplary); between-games refuel (recalculated against ISSN 2017 / Craven 2021 in the entry below). **Drift 1 — FIXED (athlete-facing overstatement):** the daily-protocol Strength block showed athletes a Nordic-curl `ai_note` claiming a **"50–70% reduction in hamstring injuries"** as fact. The definitive meta-analysis (van Dyk 2019, BJSM, 8459 athletes, doi:10.1136/bjsports-2018-100045) found a risk ratio of 0.49 — **"up to 51%", "essentially halves"**. The "70%" upper bound overstated the pooled figure, and the app already used the correct "halves" framing for neuromuscular training in `daily-protocol-training-logic.js` — internally inconsistent. Corrected the athlete-facing note + the 3 evidence comments + the reference constant (`injuryRiskReduction` 0.6 → 0.5; reference-only, not consumed) to "~halves (~51%)" with the van Dyk citation. **Drift 2 — FIXED (stale claim contradicting the app's own correction):** two code comments (`daily-protocol.js` conditioning block, `daily-protocol-periodization-config.js` acwr block) still asserted **"ACWR >1.5 = 2–4× injury risk"** — the exact multiplier `acwr.js` deliberately removed as false precision (Dalen-Lorentsen 2021). Comments only (the config's `acwr` zone numbers are reference-only, not consumed — the live zones are `acwr.js`'s), so no athlete saw it, but it's a §2 doc-drift: the codebase contradicting its own walk-back. Both comments corrected to the advisory-bands framing with the Dalen-Lorentsen citation. Backend 1064 / typecheck:functions / lint:tooling / prettier green. Creatine + beta-alanine athlete copy (supplements screen) spot-checked as consistent with the ISSN position stands but not re-pulled from PubMed this pass.

- **2026-07-17 — FULLY RESOLVED (evidence recalculation done): the between-games refuel numbers now derive from the sports-nutrition consensus.** The 2026-07-13 audit entry below says the server `betweenGamesRefuel` and the client `tournament-plan.service.ts` are "evidence-consistent (both ACSM 1.0–1.2 g/kg/h + 0.3 g/kg protein)". That understates it — **they are not the same unit**. Server (`utils/nutrition-protocols.js`, canonical, cited): `CARB_G_PER_KG_PER_H: 1.0` — a **rate**, × the window capped at 4 h, so up to **4.0 g/kg cumulative**. Client (`tournament-plan.service.ts`): three **flat per-eating-occasion** literals — `1 * kg` (medium gap, line ~148), `1.25 * kg` (long gap, line ~158), `1.2 * kg` (post-last-game recovery, line ~281). Read as a rate, the 1.25 sits **outside** the cited 1.0–1.2 range; read as a per-occasion dose it is plausibly reconcilable with the server (≈1 g/kg per sitting × N sittings ≈ the server's cumulative), but **nothing states that reconciliation and nothing tests it**. Separately, `0.3 g/kg` protein is now literally in **three** places (client ×2 as bare literals, server ×1 as the named+cited `REFUEL.PROTEIN_G_PER_KG`) — a §4 "same formula, two places" violation by the letter, even though the numbers currently agree. The client **never calls** the canonical `/api/nutrition/refuel`, so the server's careful implementation is bypassed on the only screen that shows these numbers. `tests/unit/nutrition-protocols.test.js` covers the **server only** — it imports from `netlify/functions` and cannot guard the boundary. **FIXED (engineering half, 2026-07-17):** the §4 duplication is gone and **not one athlete-facing number changed**. `REFUEL` is now `export`ed from `nutrition-protocols.js` as the canonical owner; the client mirrors the one genuinely-shared constant (`PROTEIN_G_PER_KG`) in the new `angular/src/app/core/constants/nutrition.constants.ts`, and the per-occasion gap doses (previously bare `0.4`/`1`/`1.25`/`1.2` literals scattered through `tournament-plan.service.ts`) are now named, cited constants in that same file. Guarded by `tests/unit/refuel-protein-parity.test.js` (4 tests) — asserts the mirror equals the server value, and that **neither** side regresses to a bare literal. This is the `PAIN_TRIGGER_THRESHOLD`/`SORENESS_PAIN_TRIGGER` pattern, chosen deliberately over an esbuild share because the runtimes can't share an import and that test's own comment already documents this as the house answer. Proof of no behaviour change: every rendered gram figure evaluated across bodyweight 30–200 kg for all five doses (855 figures) is byte-identical old-vs-new, and the pre-existing `tournament-plan.service.spec.ts` (25 tests, which assert the rendered strings) passes untouched. The carb numbers are deliberately **not** parity-asserted across the boundary — server rate vs client per-occasion are different units, so asserting equality would itself be a bug; that reasoning now lives permanently in the header of `nutrition.constants.ts` rather than only in this log.

**RESOLVED — recalculated against the primary literature (2026-07-18), option (2) chosen.** The client's flat per-meal literals were the drift; the server's per-hour rate model is the evidence-correct one, so the client now derives its medium/long-gap carbs from the same rate. The consensus, from PubMed:
  - **ISSN position stand** (Kerksick 2017, [DOI](https://doi.org/10.1186/s12970-017-0189-4)): rapid recovery (<4 h) → **1.2 g/kg/h**, high-GI, aggressive.
  - **Systematic review + meta-analysis, 29 needle-biopsy trials** (Craven 2021, [DOI](https://doi.org/10.1186/s40798-020-00297-0)): CHO ~**1.0 g/kg/h** maximises resynthesis, and **dosing frequency correlates positively with the rate** — spread it out, don't bolus. Also: **co-ingested protein does NOT increase glycogen resynthesis once carbohydrate is adequate.**
  - Betts & Williams 2010 ([DOI](https://doi.org/10.2165/11536900-000000000-00000)): "≥1 g/kg/h at 15–30 min intervals". Gonzalez 2017 ([DOI](https://doi.org/10.3390/nu9040344)): glucose absorption saturates ~1.2 g/min → GI distress above it. (According to PubMed.)

  **What changed:** the `medium`/`long` gap carbs are now `1.0 g/kg/h × min(gap, 4 h) × kg` — the same formula the server already used — with copy that states the per-hour figure and "dose frequently". The old flat literals **under-advised**: an 80 kg athlete with a 3 h gap went from a displayed **100 g** to **240 g** (medium ~2 h: 80 g → 160 g). In an app whose explicit stance is anti-under-fuelling (the RED-S screen's `FORBIDDEN_OUTPUT` guard), under-advising carbohydrate was the wrong-direction error, so this correction only ever **raises** the medium/long numbers, never lowers them (verified across 30–200 kg × representative gaps). **What deliberately did NOT change:** the two short windows (`turnaround` <30 min flat 12 g, `short` <75 min 0.4 g/kg) stay GI-limited — within ~75 min of the next kickoff gut tolerance is the binding constraint, not resynthesis, and the evidence agrees (Gonzalez 2017). The 4 h cap is preserved. Protein stays 0.3 g/kg but its rationale is corrected in-code: it's for muscle repair/MPS (ISSN 0.25–0.40 g/kg/feeding), **not** glycogen (Craven 2021 shows no glycogen benefit once carbs are adequate). Post-final-whistle recovery stays 1.2 g/kg (the ISSN upper rate applied to a 1 h window — the one moment the aggressive figure is fully justified, nothing downstream constrains the gut). The `1.25` long-gap literal that this entry previously (correctly) noted was not a typo is now simply gone — superseded by the rate. All figures + citations live in the `nutrition.constants.ts` header. New coverage: `tournament-plan.service.spec.ts` +2 (medium-gap 187 g derivation, 4 h cap at 320 g); `refuel-protein-parity.test.js` now guards the rate + window-cap mirror too, not just protein. The client still doesn't *call* `/api/nutrition/refuel`, but it no longer needs to — both sides now compute the identical model from the identical shared constants, which the parity test enforces.

- **2026-07-17 — FIXED: header/§2 stats drift + the whole dead `src/css` build pipeline + 3 dead artillery scripts.** (a) **Doc drift (§2 violation, partly self-inflicted):** this file's header + §2 still claimed 176 tables / 261 migrations / 117-exercised-4-orphaned / "verified 2026-07-13". Live is 178 tables (the 2026-07-16 cycle migration — whose own entry below flagged the snapshot regen as an outstanding "mechanical follow-up"; that is now done) and 263 migrations; exercised/orphaned moved to 118/3 when `auth-reset-password.js` was deleted **earlier the same day in this same session** — which should have updated this file in that commit and didn't. Header, §2, and the types-regen note now match measured live. (b) **Dead CSS pipeline removed:** `sass:compile` pointed at **four** inputs that no longer exist (`scss/tokens/design-system-tokens.scss`, `scss/{components,pages,utilities}/index.scss` — the tokens file was renamed to `_tokens.scss`+`_screen-palettes.scss` long ago, the three index files are simply gone), and `sass:watch` (`scripts/sass-watch.mjs`) restated the same dead paths. All of it emitted into `src/css`, which **does not exist**, which nothing imports, and which `design-system-check.sh` **Decision 23 explicitly forbids importing** ("No imports from generated src/css") — Angular builds its own CSS from `styles.scss` via `ng build`. `build:css` (`scripts/build-css.js`) bundled `main.css`+`tokens.css` **from that never-created directory**. Removed `sass:compile`/`sass:watch`/`build:css` + deleted `scripts/{sass-watch.mjs,build-css.js,add-generated-css-banner.js}` (the banner script was only ever invoked by the broken `sass:compile`). (c) **Dead load-test scripts removed:** all 3 `test:load:logging*` — `artillery` is **not a dependency** (neither deps nor devDeps) and `artillery-logging-test.yml` does not exist. None of the 6 removed scripts were in CI or referenced by any other script; `scripts/README.md`'s `build-css.js` row removed to match. Same class, same evidence bar, and same remedy as the 2026-07-13 dead-`seed:*` cleanup logged below. package.json valid (97 scripts). **Verified clean, NOT touched:** `netlify.toml` — all **53** distinct function targets resolve to a real file, 0 dead (the 2026-07-13 entry's "52 routes" is now 53 after `cycle.js`); all 3 e2e specs the `test:e2e:*` scripts name **do** exist (they `cd angular` first — a root-relative check false-positives them).
- **2026-07-16 — SHIPPED: female cycle module (v3 M3, §4) — special-category, private by construction, DISABLED by default.** New `cycle.js` (`/api/cycle`, own redirects) + `angular/src/app/cycle/` + tables `cycle_tracking_profiles` + `cycle_logs` (migration `20260716120000_...`, applied live via Supabase MCP; **owner-only RLS, zero staff policy**; security advisors clean). **Privacy architecture (V3-DESIGN §4):** the phase is computed by a pure, unit-tested client function `estimateCycle` — the server **never** computes or stores a phase; hormonal contraception → phase null; < 2 logged cycles → low confidence + tentative (no textbook-28 certainty, Law #7). **Cycle data NEVER enters the training engine** — no `PeriodizationInputs` field; what adapts training is the athlete's own wellness check-in (unchanged pipeline). Advisory is **inform-only** (a tested `FORBIDDEN_CYCLE_ADVICE` regex asserts no phase message ever prescribes/restricts training, across all 28 cycle days). **No staff surface exists.** Off by default; enabling records explicit versioned consent (`CYCLE_CONSENT_VERSION = 2026-07-16.v1`, GDPR Art. 9(2)(a)); **U18 force-disabled** (`ageYearsFromUserMetadata`); withdrawal = hard-delete of logs+profile + `privacy_audit_log` (typed-DELETE confirm). Cycle data included in the GDPR export bundle (`data-export.js` `cycle_profile`+`cycle_logs` — the ONLY reader outside the module). **ESLint `no-restricted-imports` forbids importing the cycle module outside `cycle/`** (§4.5 containment). Tests: `cycle.logic.spec.ts` (10, incl. the all-days inform-only guard) + `cycle.component.spec.ts` (5: U18 gate, consent gate, versioned-consent-on-enable, advisory render, typed-DELETE wipe). Linked from More → Recovery & health as "Cycle (private)". **NOTE:** live schema changed — `docs/generated/live-schema.snapshot.json` + `supabase-types.ts` regen is a mechanical follow-up (the app hand-types the two cycle interfaces, same as `external_load_metrics`). **This completes the v3/audit program build-out — `docs/v3/V3-DESIGN.md` is now deleted (its roadmap is fully shipped or explicitly deferred with reasons above).**

- **2026-07-16 — SHIPPED: Fuelling check (EA/RED-S self-screen, anti-harm — audit backlog item).** New `angular/src/app/fuelling-check/` (`app-fuelling-check`, route `/fuelling-check`, in More → Recovery & health, and linked from the result to the Fuel screen). The safety behaviour is a pure, **exhaustively-tested** module `fuelling-check.logic.ts` with a hard anti-harm contract: (1) it NEVER suggests a caloric deficit / weight loss / eating less — a `FORBIDDEN_OUTPUT` regex is asserted against the advice of **every** answer combination × youth/adult (deliberate restriction is reframed as the RED-S *risk*, never endorsed); (2) it NEVER diagnoses — every real flag sets `routeToHuman` and the UI routes the athlete to the team's nutrition/medical staff (no AI substitute); (3) youth (<18, from `ageYearsFromUserMetadata`) get a fuelling-FOR-GROWTH frame (more fuel, never less). Male-appropriate item set (16+ male club; LEAF-Q menstrual items don't apply — general RED-S CAT / low-EA signs). Tests: `fuelling-check.logic.spec.ts` (9, incl. the all-combinations forbidden-output guard) + `fuelling-check.component.spec.ts` (5). - **2026-07-15 — SHIPPED: C6 within-athlete readiness baselines (audit §4.2 — the last calculation-tier audit item).** New `utils/readiness-baseline.js`: `computePersonalCutoffs` — z-cuts on the athlete's trailing 28-day `readiness_scores` distribution (low `mean − 1.5σ`, high `mean + 0.5σ`) shrunk toward the 55/75 priors by `w = n/(n+14)`; < 10 observations → priors verbatim (Law #7); σ floor 5, clamps low∈[40,65] / high∈[65,85], min gap 10 (a flat or extreme history can't produce a degenerate ruler). `calc-readiness` classifies level/suggestion on the personal cuts, returns `baseline {...}`, and the calibration note honestly names which ruler scored today — closing the "teams should calibrate" TODO the docs carried since day one. **The engine's day-0 demotion deliberately keeps the ABSOLUTE 55 floor** (`READINESS_LOW`) — personalization adds relative sensitivity (a chronically-high athlete's deload flag fires EARLIER), never removes the absolute safety net (documented layering in CALC §3). Tests `tests/unit/readiness-baseline.test.js` (7: prior fallback, high/low-baseline direction, shrinkage, σ-floor, clamps+gap, dirty input). Backend 1053 green.
- **2026-07-15 — FIXED: onboarding production blockers (real-player report, screenshots).** Three intertwined failures stopped new players from completing profiles. **(a) Only one team existed** — the picker was honest but the DB had just International Frogs; `Domestic Frogs` + `Developing Frogs` created live (approved, Ljubljana, same coach/colors — idempotent SQL, via Supabase MCP). **(b) The signup confirmation email linked to localhost** — `landing.component` passed no `emailRedirectTo`, so Supabase used the project Site URL (localhost). Confirmation still succeeded server-side (verified: the affected player's `email_confirmed_at` is set — the token confirms BEFORE the redirect), but the dead-end page made players give up. Now signup passes `${origin}/login` (matching the existing resend flow). **REQUIRES ONE MANUAL STEP:** Supabase Dashboard → Auth → URL Configuration: set Site URL to the prod origin and add `/login`, `/update-password` to Additional Redirect URLs — code-side `emailRedirectTo` is only honored when allow-listed. **(c) The failing "Couldn't save your profile" screen came from a MONTH-OLD cached bundle** — that exact copy existed only 2026-06-03→07-10 (`git log -S`), yet a player saw it on 07-14: NGSW stages new versions in the background but a long-lived phone tab never cold-starts, and NOTHING subscribed to SwUpdate. Fixes: an app initializer (VERSION_READY → reload at the next route change; visibilitychange → checkForUpdate — the long-lived-tab healer), plus netlify.toml header guards so `custom-sw.js`/`ngsw-worker.js`/`ngsw.json`/`index.html` never inherit the year-immutable `/**/*.js` rule. Already-stuck players need ONE full tab/PWA close+reopen to pick up the new machinery; from then on updates self-heal. Old-bundle athlete (samuelwaters25@) has a confirmed account with no membership — the app will route him back into onboarding on next login, where all three teams now appear.

Week index from `mesocycleWeekFor(season_calendar, date)` (exported, shared client/server — weeks since the active off-/pre-season window start, mod 4; recurring windows resolve their cycle start; non-build phases → null → no wave). **(b) Sprint-exposure floor (§3.2, "speed vaccine"):** new input `daysSinceHighSpeed` (from the recent-sessions lookback, widened 4→**14 days** on both sides — inert for CNS spacing, which keys on timestamps); ≥ 7 days without high-speed exposure (sprint/mixed session, practice, or game per `isHighCnsSessionType`) AND none planned → the earliest technical/mobility slot becomes a **maintenance** sprint day (≤ 60 min, ≤ 5 relaxed builds); guard demotions stand and the day then carries honest "exposure postponed — add 4-6 relaxed strides when fresh" advice. Dalen-Lorentsen ACWR cluster-RCT PMID 33036995; Vázquez-Villarreal PMID 42043072; Brna PMID 40214269; Locke PMID 40145666; + Balachandran PMID 40810127 — fingers are the #1 female injury site too). **(a) NMT/ACL prevention block (§1.3, P0):** `NMT_PREVENTION_SEGMENT` (~10 min: hop-and-stick, single-leg balance, drop-land mechanics, decel-to-stop, Copenhagen) appended to the warm-up on individual quality days (strength/sprint/mixed), default-on, compliance via the existing per-exercise check-off; 6 flag-direct KB entries seeded (`database/seed-flag-specific-evidence.sql`, applied live — kb 116→122). **(b) WBGT stricter NATA bands (C8):** caution 25.7 / scale 27.8 / relocate 30.0 (live again — was dead code) / stop 32.2; the formerly-stale in-code comment now matches the constants; heat-strain band recalibrated to [27.8,30). **(c) Readiness re-weighting (§1.1):** workload 0.35→0.25, wellness 0.30→0.35, sleep 0.20→0.25 (evidence-presets mirrored); the null-ACWR response no longer fabricates a 0 (`Math.round(null*100)` bug). **(d) Graded ACWR trust:** `computeAcwrAt` returns `confidence` high ≥14 / medium 8–13 / low <8 loaded days (C2 — binary-at-14 was permanently "low" for 2-3×/week amateurs) — low confidence is displayed but excluded from readiness scoring; and `state: "building_base"` when raw chronic < 50 AU (C3 — where the floor becomes the divisor; audit's ~150 rejected: would misclassify steady amateurs) ⇒ acwr null by design + ramp guidance instead of "ACWR 6, Critical, rest". Surfaced through calc-readiness + compute-acwr. **(e) Risk multipliers retired (§1.1):** ACWR_RISK_ZONES 1.2×/1.5×/2.0×/4.2× removed (the only RCT was null); `injuryRiskMultiplier` API fields now null; warning text dropped. **(f) No fabricated bodyweight (C7, Law #7):** `FALLBACK_BODYWEIGHT_KG` (80) removed — the default over-prescribed a 45 kg athlete's per-kg carbs/fluids by ~78%; null bodyweight ⇒ `nutrition: null` ⇒ explicit "add your weight" UI states (Today/Training hydration fallbacks `?? 3.2` also removed). **(g) finger/hand injury regions** (Locke/Balachandran: #1 adult site) added to REGION_KEYWORDS. - **2026-07-14 — SHIPPED: engine week-model recalibration (coach directive — 90-min sessions, year-round sprint days, real 5-day off-season weeks, ≤7 sessions).** Deliberate behavior change through the parity harness, follow-on to the realization overhaul below. **(a) 90-min quality sessions:** `baseTargets` sprint/strength/mixed 60/75/75 → **90 min TOTAL** (semantics now explicitly include the ~25-min warm-up + DOP block — matches the realization layer's new rest-inclusive block estimates); `BUILD_TARGET_OVERRIDES.technical` 75 → 90; the season-shaped path uses `buildTargets` for offseason days (a GPP technical day is a real session). Taper minutes scale proportionally from the new base (national front 33 → 50; B6 invariants — retention ≥ 0.9, volume −40-60% — unchanged and still asserted). **(b) 5-day weeks are now reachable:** `planWeekIntents` selection allows up to 2 adjacent training pairs (never 3 consecutive) — under the old blanket no-consecutive rule the max-5 budget was mathematically unreachable without anchors (any 5 days in 7 contain ≥2 adjacencies), so every off-season week silently became 4-on/3-off. Canonical anchor-less shape: 2-2-1. **(c) sprint is year-round:** off-season `phaseSessionModel` gains `rotation: [strength, technical, sprint, strength, mixed]` — the old "no off-season sprints" rule is retired (coach directive; sprint = hamstring-protective near-max running); rotation defers an intent that would put two high-CNS days back-to-back (technical fills). **(d) doubles ceiling:** `addSecondSessions` caps at **2 PM sessions/week** (5 days + 2 doubles = 7 sessions max) and the PM is sprint after morning strength unless a practice or planned high-CNS day sits tomorrow OR yesterday (no ~14h high-CNS stacks; previously only "practice tomorrow" was checked and there was NO weekly cap). Canonical anchor-less off-season week now: Mon strength+PM-sprint / Tue technical / Wed rest / Thu sprint / Fri strength+PM-technical / Sat rest / Sun mixed = 7 sessions, high-CNS spacing ≥66h. Port regenerated + byte-parity green; goldens/snapshots updated deliberately (only targetMinutes + the retired off-season-sprint assertions moved); backend 1007 / Angular 504 green. LOGIC §2 + CALCULATIONS §5/§5.1/§7/§11 updated same pass.
- **2026-07-14 — FIXED: daily-protocol realization — the intent now owns the gym-day block shape; warm-ups persist whole; block durations include rest (production bug report with screenshots).** Four defects, one athlete-visible mess: a "Strength session" hero rendered agility mains + field conditioning + WR stations, an 8-min 3-item sprint-prep warm-up, and "6 ex · ~10 min" for 17 working sets. **(a) Block dump:** every gym day (strength/mixed/technical intent) got the identical five-block structure — new `gymBlockPlanFor(trainingFocus)` (daily-protocol-compose) gates blocks per focus: strength → DOP isometrics + strength-led main (no conditioning/skills/plyo); conditioning ("Mixed") → DOP + plyo + reduced strength + real conditioning volume (sets `readinessSets`, bout `max(60, default_duration_seconds)`, rest 60); skill → DOP + skills-led main (6 drills). **(b) DOW template hijack:** `training_session_templates` ran on ANY non-practice day (the Tuesday agility template landed on a strength day, on top of the gym blocks) — new `templateMatchesFocus` gate: template only when its `session_type` matches the day's focus family, never on low-load days, never stacked onto generated gym blocks; skips are logged (`daily_protocol_session_template_skipped`). **(c) Warm-up decimation/renaming:** `protocol_exercises.exercise_id` was NOT NULL and both persistence paths (RPC `generate_protocol_transactional` `WHERE exercise_id IS NOT NULL`, `createProtocolWithoutRpc` filter) silently dropped every template item without a keyword match — the correct 16-item gym warm-up (Jump Rope → Bike → planks…) persisted as 3 leftovers whose DISPLAY names came from mismatched library rows ("Pogo Jumps" → "Low Pogo + Ankling Prep Ladder", seeded 2026-07-12). Migration `20260714120000_protocol_exercises_names_and_rest` (applied live via Supabase MCP): +`exercise_name`, +`rest_seconds`, `exercise_id` nullable; RPC recreated to carry both and keep null-id rows; `findWarmupMatch` now whole-word + shortest-name-wins and the warm-up candidate query excludes `subcategory='team_warmup'`. Verified end-to-end against live (synthetic 2030-01-01 protocol: null-id "Jump Rope"/"Dead Bug" rows persisted with names + rest, then deleted; cascade clean). **(d) Fake durations:** the block estimate ignored rest entirely — `createBlock` now computes `Σ sets × (work + rest)` for set-based blocks (persisted `rest_seconds`, else per-block defaults) and item-total sums for continuous blocks (see CALCULATIONS §5.1). Snapshot + `supabase-types.ts` + docs:regen refreshed. Tests: `tests/unit/daily-protocol-focus-realization.test.js` (18: plans, template gate, warm-up matcher, estimator honesty incl. the exact reported cases). Backend 1007 green; lint/prettier clean. **Note:** already-persisted protocols (incl. the reported 2026-07-14 one) keep their old shape until regenerated — delete the day's `daily_protocols` row post-deploy to regenerate. **Next (approved direction, separate engine PR):** 90-min quality-session targets (warm-up + DOP included), sprint days in the off-season rotation, 5-training-day anchor-less weeks (one adjacent low/high-CNS pair allowed — the "max 5 active days" budget is currently unreachable without anchors, always yielding 4+3), up to 7 sessions/week via AM-strength/PM-sprint doubles, tournament-proximity reductions unchanged.

- **2026-07-13 — SHIPPED: "log your practice" nudge (honest fix for the "practices don't feed ACWR" gap).** Audit of the old A2 finding ("team practices contribute 0 to ACWR") showed it **overstated the gap**: `utils/acwr.js#computeSessionLoad` counts **any** logged session and practices ARE logged as `session_type='flag_football'` (live rows), so a logged practice **already feeds ACWR**; and `periodization-engine.ts addSecondSessions` already **skips practice days** (`if (teamPracticeFlags[i]) return p`). The only real gap is an **unlogged** practice — and injecting a *fabricated estimated* practice load into ACWR is exactly what §4 forbids ("load is never fabricated from defaults"). So the honest fix is a **nudge**, not a calc change: new pure `angular/src/app/today/unlogged-practice.ts#resolveUnloggedPractice(trainingDays, loggedAt, now)` detects a today/yesterday practice day with no logged session (client has both — `schedule.snapshot().trainingDays` + `periodization.recentSessions()` = last 4d completed sessions); Today shows a clock-iconed banner "Log yesterday's practice so your training load (ACWR) stays accurate" linking to `/training`. Suppressed once a session is logged for that day. `unlogged-practice.spec.ts` (7). Angular 504 / build (strict templates) / typecheck / lint green.

- **2026-07-13 — SHIPPED: Periodization Phase 5b — cooler-hour time-shift ("train at 20:00 when it drops to ~27°").** Completes Phase 5. **Audit:** the 5a heat guard already told the athlete to "train in the cooler hour" but `weather.js:115` fetched `current=` **only**, so the app had no hourly data to name that hour. **Fix:** `weather.js` now also pulls Open-Meteo **hourly** (`&hourly=…&forecast_days=2`, venue-local via `timezone=auto`), flattened by `parseHourly` into `{time,tempC,humidityPct,…}` and surfaced on `/api/weather` as `hourly[]`. New engine model: `WeatherInput.hourly`, `HourlyWeatherPoint`, `WeatherTimeShift`, `DailyPrescription.timeShift`, `PeriodizationInputs.preferredTrainingHour`. New pure `findCoolerHour(hourly, fromHour, currentWbgt)` — evaluates WBGT at the athlete's training hour; if that hour is hot (≥ WBGT_REDUCE 30°) and a **materially cooler (≥1.5°), comfortable (< 30°)** hour exists later the **same day** (within a 6h wait, before a 21:00 evening cap), returns the **earliest** such hour with an athlete-facing message. `prescribeFor` attaches `timeShift` for an outdoor field session (advisory only — the prescribed load is unchanged; skipped when injury forced a change or the day is rest). **No fabricated defaults:** no hourly / training-hour already fine / no cooler hour → no suggestion. **Anchor:** server reads `team_training_days.time` (`parseTrainingHour` "HH:MM"→hour, venue-local, correct vs the server's UTC clock); client uses the current wall-clock hour (day-0 `date` is midnight). UI: Today's session card shows a clock-iconed banner with `timeShift.message`. **Parity:** no hourly in any fixture → `timeShift` never set → drift-parity + golden **byte-identical**; new `weather-timeshift.test.js` (7) locks `findCoolerHour` (suggests earliest comfortable hour / already-fine → null / hot-all-evening → null / past-cap → null / no-data → null) + a `prescribeFor` integration. Port regenerated; backend **989** / Angular **497** / build (strict templates) / typechecks / lint green. **Phase 5 (weather) COMPLETE.**
- **2026-07-13 — SHIPPED: Periodization Phase 5a — weather guard rebuilt on WBGT × duration × intensity.** The gated audit's Phase 5 (weather), part a. **Audit:** the guard keyed on INTENT not heat strain (`OUTDOOR_INTENSE = {sprint,mixed,taper-prime}`, `periodization-engine.ts` guard) — so a **75-min outdoor skills session at 33°C was completely unguarded** while short sprints were scaled (the D13 inversion); thresholds were raw apparent-temp (28/32/35/38°C) not WBGT; the scale action was a **flat 20% cut** regardless of session length; and humidity (fetched by `weather.js:115` as `relative_humidity_2m`) was **dropped** before the guard. **Fix:** new `approxWBGT(tempC, humidityPct)` (Australian BoM shade formula `0.567·Ta + 0.393·e + 3.94`, cited) → the guard now uses **WBGT** with NATA/ACSM continuous-activity thresholds (27.8 scale / 30.0 relocate / 32.2 stop °C WBGT) when humidity is present, guards **all field intents incl. `technical`** (fixes the inversion), and **strain-scales the volume cut** by WBGT-band × duration × intensity (a long sprint in heat loses up to 50%, a short skills session ~20%). Humidity threaded end-to-end: `WeatherInput.humidityPct` (new) ← client `periodization.service` (`/api/weather` `d.humidity`) + server `resolveWeather` (`data.humidity`). **No fabricated defaults:** humidity absent → byte-identical **legacy apparent-temp fallback**, and the athlete sees "29°C WBGT" vs "33°C feels-like" honestly. **Parity:** the drift-parity weather fixtures (humidity-less) stay **byte-identical**; new `tests/unit/weather-wbgt-guard.test.js` (7) locks the formula, the humidity-flips-risk case (30°C @ 40% benign vs @ 70% STOP), the technical-now-guarded inversion, and the duration×intensity scaling. Port regenerated; backend 982 / Angular 497 / typechecks / lint green. **Phase 5b (next, pending):** Open-Meteo **hourly** forecast + the **time-shift** recommendation ("train at 20:00 when it drops to ~27°C") — the reason text already says "train in the cooler hour" but the app has no hourly data yet to name it.
- **2026-07-13 — HOTFIX: weather-provider failure 500'd `/api/periodization-prescription`.** Live production 500 on Today/Training (blanked the athlete's plan). Root cause: `getWeatherData` returns `null` on ANY provider/geocoding failure (`weather.js:166` — Open-Meteo / Nominatim timeout/error), but `resolveWeather` then dereferenced `data.temp` on that null → `TypeError` → the assembly `Promise.all` rejected → the whole prescription 500'd. Weather is a **non-critical guard** input; a provider hiccup must degrade to "no weather", never take down the plan. **Fix:** guard the null (`!data || data.temp == null`) + wrap `resolveWeather` in a `try/catch` that logs (`periodization_weather_resolve_failed`) and returns `null`, so neither a null result nor an unexpected throw (home-city geocoding, provider) can 500 the endpoint. `tests/unit/periodization-weather-resilience.test.js` reproduces the crash and locks null/throw/geocode-throw → resolves-null, real weather → mapped. Shipped as a standalone hotfix (PR #204, off `main`) so it reached production immediately, decoupled from the gated Phase 4 taper PR (#203, which also carries it). Backend 975 / functions-typecheck / lint green.
- **2026-07-13 — SHIPPED: Periodization Phase 4b — two-layer, level-graduated taper (`taper_rules` wired).** Follow-on to 4a below, resolving the §4 issue that `taper_rules` (curated, Bosquet/Mujika-cited) was **unwired**. **Two-layer model** (user-directed): the engine is deterministic and runs the taper on a **materialized `TaperRuleset`** — never raw DB rows. Layer 1 (engine): `EMBEDDED_TAPER_RULES` (version `v1-2026-07-13`, mirrors the live table) is the pure/offline default; `taperLevelFor` maps the 7-value `CompetitionLevel`→ the curated 5 (club→local, continental→international, olympic→world, unknown→national); `resolveTaperTargets(ruleset, level, isFinalThird)` holds intensity at `baseline×intensityRetention` (RPE≥7, never mobility) and cuts VOLUME by the level's `volumeFloorPct` with a deeper final-third (×0.66). Layer 2 (server): `periodization-prescription.js#resolveTaperRuleset` reads active `taper_rules`, materializes them into the **same** schema (source `live`), passes via `inputs.taperRuleset`; **falls back to embedded** on any error / partial-vocabulary / malformed number (never a bad taper). Response carries `taperPolicy {version, source}` for provenance. So the taper is now **graduated by event level** — a local game (floor 0.70/ret 0.90 → RPE7, 42min) tapers far less than a World peak (floor 0.50/ret 1.00 → RPE8, 30min), and the national default = 33min/6reps front, 22min/4reps final (this supersedes 4a's flat international-tier numbers below). Migration `taper_rules_version_column` added `version` (default `v1-2026-07-13`) so live/embedded stay in lock-step; live introspection confirms **live == embedded == the golden test** (0 behavior change from wiring, pure additive provenance + future coach-tunability seam — the resolver already accepts an override ruleset). Tests: `taper-two-layer.test.js` (level map + embedded golden + resolver graduation/retention/min-reps-floor/override) + `resolveTaperRuleset` hydration cases (full→live, partial→null, malformed→null, error→null) in `periodization-prescription.test.js`; drift-parity gained a level-graduation case. Port regenerated; snapshot+types+docs:regen refreshed (176 tables, 0 DRIFT). Backend 971 / Angular 497 / functions-typecheck / lint all green.
- **2026-07-13 — FIXED: Periodization Phase 4a — the taper now holds INTENSITY and cuts VOLUME (rubric B6).** The gated periodization audit's Phase 4 (graduated gameday ramp + taper). **Audit finding:** the two ramp axes are structurally sound — the season macro-phase (Phase 3 `phaseSessionModel`: offseason=GPP → preseason=build → inseason=maintain → peak=game-speed) and the game-proximity micro-phase (`schedule-resolver.ts` `resolvePhase`: transition >14d → accumulation 2–14d → taper → taper-prime ≤24h → competition, importance-tiered so a regular league game gets a 2-day mini-taper while world/olympic events get the 7–14-day full taper). **But the taper itself VIOLATED rubric item 6** ("volume −40–60%, intensity MAINTAINED or raised — if any taper path reduces intensity, flag as VIOLATES"): against the accumulation `sprint` baseline (RPE 8 / 60 min / 10 reps, `periodization-engine.ts:1917-1923`), every taper path **cut intensity** — `TAPER_CONFIG.individual.regular` was RPE 6, `.final` was **`mobility` RPE 4** (deleting all CNS/velocity work in the final 48h — the textbook detraining error, Mujika & Padilla 2003), and `PRACTICE_PHASE_MODIFIERS.taper`/`taper_final` were RPE 6/5 (base 7). **Fix (intensity held at baseline, only volume cut):** `individual.regular` → sprint, **RPE 8**, 30 min, 5 reps (−50% vol); `individual.final` → **sprint** (not mobility), **RPE 8**, 20 min, 3 reps (progressive, deeper volume cut, still near-max efforts); practice `taper`/`taper_final` → **RPE 7** held, minutes 90→60/45. `taper-prime` (≤24h game-eve primer) left as-is — it already keeps velocity strides (4 reps) and is the opener, not a taper block. **Parity harness:** `tests/unit/periodization-drift-parity.test.js` gained a Phase-4 block with B6-invariant assertions (velocity preserved / RPE ≥ baseline / volume −40-60% / progressive) that FAILED before and PASS after; the two non-taper golden snapshots stayed **byte-identical** (git diff = 64 insertions, 0 deletions) proving no collateral drift; the `phase-taper` golden fixture in `periodization-parity.test.js` updated to the new values. Port regenerated (`build:periodization-engine`) + source⇔port parity green. Backend 949 / Angular typecheck (node@22) / lint all green.

Angular lint clean. **Doc-drift sweep (same day):** checked the full doc set against live — `scripts/README.md` still listed the 9 deleted `seed:*` commands (fixed → now points to the current SQL-seed mechanism), and this file's own header + §2 still claimed **181 base tables / 249 migrations / regenerated 2026-07-08** while its generated `DATA_MODEL.md` correctly said 176 (fixed → 176 tables, 261 migration files, all generated docs + `supabase-types.ts` regenerated 2026-07-13). - **2026-07-13 — FIXED: nutrition-endpoint UI wired (caffeine single-sourced, batch-testing surfaced).** Two of the three new nutrition endpoints now have a UI home on the Supplements screen: **(1) caffeine⇄sleep** — the component carried its OWN divergent client heuristic (fixed 3 mg/kg, 8 h cutoff); retired it and wired `caffeineTiming` to the server `GET /api/supplements/caffeine-timing` (per-kg 3-6 mg/kg dose, 6 h Drake-2013 cutoff), keyed on the first game (pre-game dose) and last game (top-up sleep guard) — one caffeine decision, server-owned, no drift. **(2) batch-testing** — new "Batch-testing & safety" section renders `GET /api/supplements/safety` (the strict-liability imperative + per-stack contamination-risk tiers). **(3) between-games refuel** — deliberately NOT given a new card: the client already surfaces it well in the gameday **Tournament timeline** (`tournament-plan.service.ts`, gap-classified per-kg 0.4-1.25 g/kg + 0.3 g/kg protein, low-fat/fibre short gaps — the same ACSM evidence as the server `betweenGamesRefuel`); a redundant card would be a 3rd drifting surface.
