- ~~**Two migration directories.**~~ **Fixed 2026-06-25** — `database/migrations/` (172 files, 0% overlap with applied history) deleted entirely. `supabase/migrations/` is now the single tracker, reconciled against the live project's 169 applied versions (Supabase MCP `list_migrations`): 9 files renamed to match their actual applied timestamp (the `athlete_personal_events` case and 8 others — divergence was worse than the "4 recent cases" originally logged here), and 9 applied migrations that had **no local file at all** (applied directly to prod, never committed — the whole `prescription_templates`/`readiness_gates`/`weather_substitution_rules`/`contraindication_rules`/`taper_rules`/`prescription_audit_log`/`rtp_prescription_approvals`/`injury_gate`/`qb_throw_au`/`season_phases` feature plus `widen_readiness_scores_subscores` and `add_recovery_block_load_columns`) were reconstructed from live schema introspection (columns/indexes/FKs/RLS policies) and committed under their correct applied version. Remaining local-only files all predate `20260130125835` (the first applied/tracked migration) — expected, not drift.
- **Committed secrets.** Old `BACKEND_SETUP`/`LOCAL_DEVELOPMENT` docs contained real-looking Supabase JWTs (now deleted with those docs) — rotate if they were ever real.
- **Auth leaked-password (HIBP) advisor** is unresolved unless the native GoTrue setting is enabled (see §7 auth hardening).
- **2026-06-09 audit — resolved same day** (cross-team session mutation fix: see Ledger):
  - ~~Weather fake location~~ **Fixed**: `weather.js` resolves the caller's team `home_city` (geocoded) when no coords passed; no location → explicit `available:false`, never a default city. Client guard skips on unavailable. San Francisco defaults deleted. Team home_city is set via `teams.home_city` (no settings UI yet — set via SQL/roster admin).
  - ~~No unique constraint on training_sessions natural key~~ **Fixed**: `training_sessions_user_date_type_key` UNIQUE (user_id, session_date, session_type) + `log_training_session` RPC now ON CONFLICT upserts (COALESCE, non-destructive). Migration `20260609192100`.
  - ~~Periodization phase from global `is_active` program~~ **Fixed**: `training-plan.js` + `smart-training-recommendations.js` resolve via the athlete's `player_programs` assignment (prefers `current_phase_id`).
  - ~~Fabricated RPE in load math~~ **Fixed**: `training-plan.js`/`daily-training.js` ACWR now delegate to canonical `utils/acwr.js` (workload ?? rpe×min, missing → excluded). Client `acwr.service` excludes load-less sessions instead of zero-counting.
  - ~~Client ACWR EWMA divergence~~ **Fixed**: client mirrors server (oldest→newest fold, uncoupled 21d chronic, λ=2/(N+1)); evidence presets updated.
  - ~~RPE null-overwrite on resubmit~~ **Fixed**: update branch only writes provided fields, workload recomputed from merged (new ?? saved) pair.
  - ~~`compute-acwr` multi-team `maybeSingle()`~~ **Fixed**: staff-team × athlete-membership intersection across ALL memberships.
- **2026-06-09 audit — closed out (second pass):**
  - ~~Multi-team `limit(1)`/`maybeSingle()` in coach-core + team-activity-resolver~~ **Fixed**: `getCoachTeamId` is deterministic (most recent active membership) and accepts an explicit team — `?teamId=` on the coach GET lanes, `teamId` in POST payloads — validated against the caller's staffed memberships. `team-activity-resolver` searches activities across ALL of the athlete's active teams (old `maybeSingle()` errored on >1 membership → silent "no activity"); newest created activity wins.
  - ~~No spec tests for injury/CNS guards~~ **Fixed**: `prescribeFor — injury guard` suite added (severe/moderate/minor scaling, no-op cases, competition-day immunity, injury-beats-weather precedence); CNS spacing suite landed with the feature commit.
  - **Orphaned ghost-lane tests removed** (officials/equipment/depth-chart/scouting/program-cycles/season-reports/tournament-calendar/push ×10 files + absence-request & research-sync cases); stale `user-profile` pg-Pool mock rewritten for the supabase client.

- **2026-06-10 — engine personalization & safety pass (athlete-driven brief):** the prescription engine is now age-, position-, region-, season- and travel-aware. All changes push in the _safe_ direction (more conservative); adversarially verified.
  - **Nutrition**: `CARB_PER_KG` periodised to energy expenditure — a short sprint day is 4.5 g/kg (was a flat 6 → 480g for 80kg), pre-game/game-day stay elevated (6/7). Heat now adds +0.5L fluid (was documented, never applied).
  - **Injury regions** (`athlete-injuries.js restrictionsFor`): added **soleus** + patella/adductor/tibialis/plantar/ITB/tendon to lower-limb, a **core/trunk** category, and an **upper-body** category; an unrecognised region now **fails safe** (restricts sprint + high-intensity + throwing). The client injury guard derives `restrictsThrowing` so an upper-body issue pulls throwing/snapping without touching running.
  - **Age** (`cnsRecoveryHoursForAge`): CNS recovery window <35→48h, 35–39→60h, 40+→72h (monotonic, floored, from `date_of_birth`).
  - **Position** (`withPositionEmphasis`): QB/WR-DB/center accessory-prehab emphasis (additive only — never changes intent/load), surfaced on Today; throwing-restriction overrides QB/center into a protect-the-arm message.
  - **Tournament congestion**: `peakDayGameCount` now a conservative worst-day estimate (`ceil(games/(days-1))`, not the average — averaging fail-open fixed); ≥3 games/day trips heavy-density de-load regardless of the 10/14d total.
  - **Season phases**: added **peak** (sharp/low-volume) and **postseason** (regeneration); split seasons (multiple windows incl. a mid-season off gap) resolve per day via `macroPhaseFor`. Onboarding picker offers Off/Pre/In/Peak/Post-season.
  - **Travel**: `daily_wellness_checkin.travel_hours` + extended `upsert_wellness_checkin` RPC + a wellness chip control + a bounded **readiness penalty** in `calc-readiness` (1–2h −2, 3–5h −4, ≥6h −8; subtract-only).
  - **Knowledge base**: 30 → **104** evidence-based, merlin-approved entries (hamstrings, knees, glutes, core, isometrics→plyometrics, warm-ups, rest, age bands, QB/WR/DB/center demands, travel, nutrition periodisation). Adult-male-16+ only.
  - Calibration constants (carb g/kg, CNS hours, congestion threshold, travel penalty) are documented in-code as **starting points** for the coach to tune against team history.
  - **Per-game ACWR load**: `calc-readiness` now injects an estimated per-game internal load (~350 AU, tunable) for PAST games from the schedule spine into the daily-load map (multi-day events spread across days; per-day MAX(logged, estimate) so it never lowers a day and avoids double-count), so a tournament's acute load/ACWR rises instead of reading falsely safe.
  - ~~**Open / deferred:** QB throw-count / center snap-count → quantified load gating (needs the throw/snap logs wired + the coach's dosing thresholds)~~ **Reassessed 2026-06-25**: not actually a "needs building" gap — `netlify/functions/qb-throwing.js` (424 lines, arm-care endpoint, writes `training_sessions.throw_au`/`throw_count`) and the `api.service.ts` route table already exist but have **zero callers anywhere in the Angular app** — orphaned, same pattern as the ExerciseDB lane in §4. No UI ever logs a throw count, so there's no data for the engine to gate on regardless of dosing-threshold decisions. Region-specific prehab/return-to-play _progression_ engine (the knowledge base covers the education); season leap-day (Feb 29) + user-declared overlapping windows are minor edge cases. `ensure_public_user_profile` has a pre-existing `last_name` NOT NULL quirk for email-only auth users (affects all wellness writes for such users).

- **2026-06-10 — architecture refactor + COMPOSE wiring (two engines clarified):** an audit confirmed `periodization.service.ts` (Angular, **intent/targets** — Today/Training/Gameday) and `daily-protocol.js` (backend, **exercise realization**) are TWO engines. Decision: **compose** (intent layer authoritative, daily-protocol realizes exercises for it). Do NOT delete daily-protocol (a 10-module, routed, tested engine; the earlier "dead code" read was wrong).
  - **Class 3 (config extraction, behaviour-neutral):** `TAPER_CONFIG` (taper windows/targets centralised); `baseTargets` (in-season) vs `BUILD_TARGET_OVERRIDES` (pre-season heavier on light intents) — resolves audit **M1** (the divergence is deliberate, now explicit); `INJURY_RESPONSE` (injury caps). Each verified byte-identical against the 85-anchor periodization preserve-list.
  - **Class 1 / finding 1.1:** a practice day on a recovery phase is now honoured at recovery intensity (a `recovery` row in `PRACTICE_PHASE_MODIFIERS`) instead of being dropped; intent is data-driven per row.
  - **COMPOSE backend:** `daily-protocol /generate` optionally takes `{intent, intentLabel, position}`; `mapIntentToSession` + `positionFlagsFor` realize exercises for that intent and fix the `isQB`/center bug (raw `'qb'` ≠ `'quarterback'`). **Backward-compatible** — no intent → legacy path byte-unchanged (8 legacy + 8 mapping tests).
  - **COMPOSE client:** `ProtocolService` posts the intent → Training renders the realized blocks as **block-cards** (re-skin adapted from `todays-practice-redesign-mockup.html`, mapped to app tokens, responsive 1-col→2-up@768). Render contract **verified against real data** (3 protocols / 33 exercises; block_types + exercise fields match the model).
  - **Status:** all of the above is **🔧 BUILT, NOT DEPLOYED**, and the compose stack's intent→exercise _quality_ is **not yet e2e-verified** (needs a live intent-passed `/generate`). Coach guide shipped: `FlagFit-Coach-Guide.docx` (+ internal appendix).
  - **2026-06-23 closeouts:** ~~Class 1 `resolveDayType→applyModifiers` pipeline~~ **closed** — the day-type→`PRACTICE_PHASE_MODIFIERS` pipeline (incl. finding 1.1: practice on a recovery phase honoured at recovery intensity) is now **locked by 7 spec tests** in `periodization.service.spec.ts` (94/94 green). ~~Settings position-picker discoverability~~ **closed** — a primary-position picker is now in Settings → Prefs (`settings.component`), saving `primaryPosition` to `/api/player-settings` and refreshing the prescription engine; labels single-sourced from `POSITION_VOLUME`.
  - **2026-06-24 closeouts:** ~~intent→exercise _quality_ e2e verification~~ **verified** against the live stack — `/api/daily-protocol/generate` with an `intent` payload realizes a distinct, intent-correct session per intent (strength→37 ex / sprint→12 / recovery→11 / practice→`practice_day`). ~~Class 2 schema cleanup~~ **done** — `deprecate_dead_fields` phase 2 (`20260624074022`) DROPPED the two `*_deprecated` columns from phase1 after live verification of zero code/view/function/policy/index dependency (dead data: 2× stale `[1,2,4,5,6]`, 16× `0.5`). ~~deploy~~ **not a manual step** — Netlify auto-publishes `main` → production on every merge (rollback: `netlify rollback`).
  - **Still remaining:** Class 2's _deferred_ fields (`daily_routine`/`current_limitations`/`flag_practice_schedule`/`max_sessions_per_week`) stay — phase1 found them to be intended features, not dead. ~~Migration-file hygiene (the timestamp-drifted duplicate files in §6 below) is unaddressed.~~ **Partly addressed 2026-07-01** — the two concrete missing-file gaps are fixed (see below); 9 pre-2026-06 out-of-band files remain untracked in the applied ledger (see below), left as-is.

- **2026-06-23 — load-domain + design-system drift sweep (this session's PRs):**
  - **Schema-drift CI clean slate:** all 44 schema-drift findings resolved (baseline 0); the CI guard (`scripts/check-schema-drift.mjs`) blocks new drift. New tables this window: `team_activities`, `proactive_checkins`; `training_sessions.throw_au` etc.
  - **Canonical load model unified:** `coach-core.js` no longer re-implements ACWR/readiness (was a simple-ratio + fabricated `1.0`/`85`) — both coach paths now use the canonical EWMA engine (`utils/acwr.js`) and the persisted `readiness_scores`. **Consent:** coach-facing readiness is now gated by the blocked set (no longer leaks a consent-blocked athlete's score).
  - **Gameday/tournament load:** `record_event_participation` RPC now **distributes** a multi-day tournament's load across its days (was lumped on day 1) and dedups any same-date competition session, so the two gameday paths can't double-count acute load.
  - **Recovery-block cap enforced:** `buildProtocolDecisionContext` now caps `adjustedLoadTarget` by an active `recovery_blocks.max_load_percent` (was set but never applied to the prescription).
  - **`calculated_readiness` scale:** `wellness-logs.js` now writes 0–100 (was 1–10 into a 0–100 column that `ai-chat`/`coach-core` read).
  - **Week-ahead fix:** `periodization.weekAhead()` applies today's readiness/ACWR only to today (mirrors the weather guard) — future days resolve to their phase-driven plan instead of 7 frozen recovery days.
  - **Design system — Phase E token migration:** the TS color bridge (`design-tokens.util.ts`, `app.constants.ts`) + `index.html` were emitting the **old** CSS var names (`--ds-primary-green`, `--space-*`, …) that no stylesheet defines → empty strings at runtime (broken chart colors, white flash, Poppins). Migrated to Phase E tokens (`--accent`/`--good`/`--s-*`); `index.html` now links Space Grotesk + Plus Jakarta Sans (CSP updated for Google Fonts), dark-default boot CSS. Source of truth: `scss/tokens/_tokens.scss` (`--accent #00E07A`). ~~Deferred: `e2e/color-contrast.spec.ts` rework~~ **Done 2026-06-23** — the spec was reworked off the dead PrimeNG/"white-on-green" rules to enforce **WCAG AA contrast ratios** and lock the ink-on-accent pairing (mint `#00E07A` + `--on-accent` ink ≈ 11:1; white-on-mint would fail). ~~Still deferred: visual-regression baseline regen~~ **Done 2026-06-25** — all 23 baselines in `angular/e2e/__snapshots__/visual-regression.spec.ts/` re-captured against the live app (post-PrimeNG-removal, post-Phase-E tokens); fixed a stale `loginToApp` helper (`waitForURL` only matched `/dashboard|onboarding/`, app now lands on `/today` post-login) that was silently blocking the desktop-suite captures; deleted the orphaned `primeng-card.png` baseline (component no longer exists, no test referenced it).

- **2026-07-01 — travel-day feature audit + fix pass (bugs found on the unmerged `feat/travel-hotel-info` branch, before merge):**
  - **Client/server phase-resolver mismatch (Today vs. Week-Ahead):** `periodization.weekAhead()` computed day-0's phase via the client's own `resolvePhase` instead of the server-computed `snap.currentPhase` the `today` signal uses — the two resolvers key off different day-of-week sources (local vs UTC) and could disagree for a few hours around UTC midnight, showing a different phase/prescription on Today vs. the first Week-Ahead tile for the same day. **Fixed**: day 0 now reuses `snap.currentPhase`.
  - **Travel day bypassed the ACWR-danger/readiness-collapse safety guards:** the travel branch returned before reaching guards explicitly commented "overrides phase — safety first" / "regardless of phase". **Fixed**: travel-day handling moved after both guards (matches how team-practice already deferred to them); 3 new spec tests lock this (`periodization.service.spec.ts` — "prescribeFor — travel day").
  - **Unreachable travel/game-day badge:** Today's and Training's hero-band badge checked `recoveryEmphasis` before `intent`, so the "travel day"/"game day" labels could never render (both intents always carry a "high"/"critical" emphasis) — Today and Training could also show different labels for the same day. **Fixed**: intent-specific labels now checked first in both components.
  - **Training-points feature silently no-op:** `training-complete.js` inserted into `user_achievements`, a read-only view — every write threw and was swallowed. **Fixed**: new `increment_training_points` RPC upserts `player_training_stats.total_points` directly (SECURITY DEFINER, service_role-only).
  - **Onboarding race condition:** `player-settings.js` did check-then-insert instead of an atomic upsert; concurrent submits could double-insert and silently drop data. **Fixed**: now calls the existing `ensure_public_user_profile` RPC (atomic INSERT…ON CONFLICT) then an unconditional UPDATE.
  - **`ensure_public_user_profile` `last_name` NULL bug — confirmed and fixed**: `last_name` derived via substring-after-first-space was NULL for any email-only auth user (no space in the source string); `users.last_name` is NOT NULL, so the profile-ensure insert threw, blocking every RPC that calls it first (`upsert_wellness_checkin`, onboarding, etc). Fixed with a `COALESCE(..., 'Account')` floor.
  - **Other fixes**: dead `travel: "morning_mobility"` video-category mapping (no such category exists) → `mobility`; staff create-event form never set `competitions.level`, so coaches had no way to flag an international/continental/world fixture → added a level picker; `wellness-checkin.js` was missing `travelHours` range validation (bad input surfaced as a 500, not 422) → added.
  - **DB hygiene**: 2 prod-applied migrations had no local file (`storage_community_media_rls`, `add_hotel_info_to_competition_events`, the latter existing locally under the wrong timestamp) — both fixed/renamed to their real applied versions; regenerating docs also surfaced `sponsors` (live + used, reconstructed) and `video_bookmarks`/`weekly_training_analysis` (live, unreferenced by any current code — left as flagged DRIFT, a keep-or-drop call for the team, not auto-dropped). 9 pre-2026-06 out-of-band local files remain untracked in the applied-migration ledger (idempotent, low blast-radius per the 2026-06-25 reconciliation) — not re-registered, since doing so without re-verifying each against live state risked writing an inaccurate ledger entry.
  - **Security/perf**: `upsert_wellness_checkin` was anon-executable via a leftover default PUBLIC grant (self-guarded, not exploitable) → revoked. `prescription_audit_log`/`rtp_prescription_approvals`/`team_season_phases` (reconstructed tables) missed the schema-wide RLS auth-initplan optimization pass → applied; `physical_measurements`'s 5 percentage columns were `numeric(4,2)` (same 100.00-overflow class as the fixed readiness-score bug) → widened to `numeric(5,2)`; `rtp_prescription_approvals.approved_by`/`daily_protocol_id` FKs were unindexed → indexed.
  - `supabase-types.ts` and `docs/generated/*` regenerated; `live-schema.snapshot.json` bumped to 2026-07-01 (this also fixed genuinely stale doc content dated 2026-06-23 that pre-dated the 2026-06-25 migration reconciliation and never got a re-regen).

- **2026-07-23 — SHIPPED: Stripe billing backend built and applied live (schema + entitlements + checkout/webhook/portal/lapse-lifecycle), broken `player_payments` manual-tracking feature retired.** Follows the payments/billing audit-and-propose pass (`docs/payments_billing_and_data_retention_proposal.md`) and an explicit "fix the gaps" instruction, scoped to both that proposal and the companion GPS/wearable one, with an explicit "no credentials yet — build it ready to wire up, nothing processes real payments yet" constraint.
  **Schema** (`20260723140000_billing_and_subscriptions.sql`): `billing_customers` (`CHECK` enforces exactly one of `owner_user_id`/`owner_team_id`, never both/neither), `subscriptions` (6 tiers × 6 statuses, `past_due_since` set once and preserved across webhook redeliveries — never bumped forward, which would keep resetting the 14-day grace clock), `invoices`, `team_members.is_billable_seat` (a column, not a hardcoded role list, so seat-billing policy can change without a migration), `seat_sync_queue` + `enqueue_seat_sync()` trigger (Postgres can't call Stripe directly; this only enqueues — nothing drains the queue yet, flagged in the Ledger row, not hidden). Trigger syntax (`AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE OF status, is_billable_seat OR DELETE`) was verified against a scratch table before applying, given this repo's history of subtle migration-syntax failures.
  **`utils/entitlements.js`** is the single tier→limits source (CLAUDE.md §4 pattern, same reasoning as the ACWR single-source-of-truth rule) — `getEntitlement()` unions an individual subscription with any active team membership's subscription, most-permissive-wins (boolean OR, numeric max, `null` = unlimited propagates). Wired into `training-sessions.js` and `daily-load.js` as the representative pattern: the effective history-cutoff is `max(requested cutoff, entitlement-derived cutoff)`, not "clamp only if the caller didn't ask for a date" — a free-tier user can't bypass the 30-day floor by simply requesting `startDate=2020-01-01`. Every call site passes its own request-scoped Supabase client explicitly (never entitlements.js's default `supabaseAdmin`), so existing tests' mocks aren't silently bypassed by a real network call. Remaining read endpoints still need the same floor applied — not done in this pass, tracked as a gap.
  **`stripe-checkout.js`/`stripe-webhook.js`/`stripe-portal.js`/`billing-lapse-check.js`** built against `utils/stripe-client.js` (a lazy-singleton wrapper, never importing the `stripe` SDK directly in a function file) so the whole backend is unit-testable with a mocked Stripe client and zero real API keys — checkout/webhook/portal all return a clean 503 "Billing is not yet available" until `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY`/`STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`/price-id env vars are actually set. The webhook handler is idempotent (every write is an upsert keyed on the Stripe object id, since Stripe redelivers events) and notifies the individual owner or every team owner/admin (never a non-admin coach) the moment a subscription first goes `past_due` — never on a redelivery of an already-known past-due state. `billing-lapse-check.js` flips `users.account_status` between `active`/`suspended` after the T&C §7.5 14-day grace window, with an explicit, tested guarantee that it never overwrites a `paused` or `deleted` account (those are higher-priority, orthogonal states) — it has no cron trigger yet, same on-demand/service-role convention as `alert-evaluate-rules.js`.
  **`player_payments` retired** (`20260723150000_drop_broken_player_payments.sql`, 0 live rows): `payments-core.js` read/wrote columns that never existed on the table and inserted rows missing two `NOT NULL` columns the table actually has — any real write through that code path would have failed outright. It was also a manual/offline Venmo-Zelle-cash tracking feature, a fundamentally different product from Stripe subscription billing, and had zero real call sites in the Angular app (`payments: "/api/payments"` in `api.service.ts` was a dead, unreferenced route constant). Deleted `payments-core.js`, its dispatch in `payments.js` (kept `/sponsors`/`/sponsor-logo`, which share the same physical function file), the `/api/payments*` `netlify.toml` redirects, the dead Angular route constant, and `payments-mutations.test.js`.
  **Verification:** security advisor re-run clean (only the expected `INFO`-level "RLS Enabled No Policy" on `seat_sync_queue`, which is intentional default-deny — nothing legitimate reads it directly); `supabase-types.ts`/`live-schema.snapshot.json`/generated docs regenerated (204 tables, was 201 — net +4 new, −1 dropped; types 0 stale/0 live-missing); backend suite 1231 tests (+~57 new across `entitlements`, `stripe-checkout`, `stripe-webhook`, `stripe-portal`, `billing-lapse-check`, and the entitlement-floor regression test), `lint:tooling`, `typecheck:functions` all green. GPS/wearable-side gaps (`docs/gps_wearable_csv_import_proposal.md`) and the remaining payments gaps (data-export/hard-deletion wiring, CSV import bridge, device OAuth framework) are tracked separately, not yet built.

- **2026-07-23 — SHIPPED: closed the 3 remaining payments-proposal gaps (`data-export.js`/`process_hard_deletion` table-list gaps, dormant `acwr_frozen`).** Continuation of the billing pass above, per `docs/payments_billing_and_data_retention_proposal.md` §8/§9's flagged items 8 and 9.
  **`data-export.js`:** added a `billing` GDPR export section (`billing_customers`/`subscriptions`/`invoices` — individual billing only, never a team's, since a team member isn't the data subject for the team's billing). This needed its own two-hop fetch (`exportBillingData`), not the generic single-`.eq()` per-table export path every other section uses, since `invoices`/`subscriptions` don't carry a direct `user_id` column. Also corrected the `retentionPolicy` copy in the `info` endpoint, which previously read as if **all** data is "retained for 3 years after account deletion" — flatly contradicting the 30-day hard-delete flow it sits next to. Now states the real split: most personal data is deleted 30 days after an account-deletion request; financial records (subscriptions/invoices) are the one exception, retained 3 years for legal/tax compliance.
  **`process_hard_deletion`:** was already correctly leaving `billing_customers`/`subscriptions`/`invoices` untouched (they were never in its deletion list, and financial-record retention requires that) — but with zero documentation of why, which the proposal flagged as a real gap: a future reader could reasonably "fix" the omission as a bug (this file names a past instance of exactly that mistake, commit `80389b2`, for a different intentional exclusion). Fixed with a documentation-only migration (`20260723160000_document_hard_deletion_billing_exclusion.sql`, `CREATE OR REPLACE` with identical logic — verified byte-for-byte against the live `pg_get_functiondef` before writing it, so this is provably a no-op on behavior) adding an explicit header comment plus an inline marker at the point in the function where those tables are conspicuously absent.
  **`acwr_frozen`:** existed on `account_pause_requests` with a `true` default since the pause feature shipped, but nothing anywhere read it — pausing only ever flipped `users.account_status`, never touched an ACWR calculation. The gap: `computeAcwrAt`'s acute/chronic windows zero-fill every day with no logged load, so a paused athlete's ratio would silently decay toward "detraining" (or trip the `building_base` return-to-training state) purely from the pause itself — not from any real training change. Fixed with one shared resolver, `utils/acwr.js#resolveAcwrEvaluationDate(supabase, userId, requestedDate)` — the single place this policy is implemented (CLAUDE.md §4: both `calc-readiness.js` and `compute-acwr.js` call it and pass its result to `computeAcwrAt`/the series builder, so it can't drift between the two ACWR consumers the way the rest of that module already guards against). It looks up the athlete's most recent active, ACWR-frozen pause and, if one exists and predates the requested date, returns the pause moment instead — **only ever earlier than requested, never later**, so this can only make a stale/paused reading more accurate, never mask a real current safety signal. `compute-acwr.js`'s 42-day series is capped the same way, so the dashboard graph doesn't show a fabricated decaying tail through a paused gap either.
  **Verification:** given ACWR is a safety-relevant calculation (CLAUDE.md §4), this got the full treatment rather than a quick patch — traced both real call sites end-to-end before writing the resolver, confirmed via the existing test suite that no other test path reaches the new query (only 2 of ~7 files importing `calc-readiness.js`/`compute-acwr.js` actually invoke their handlers; the rest import pure helper functions and are untouched). New `resolveAcwrEvaluationDate` unit tests (`acwr-freeze.test.js`, 4 cases: no pause, frozen-and-predates, frozen-but-future — never moves later, missing userId) plus 2 new end-to-end regression cases (one per call site) proving a session logged AFTER the freeze moment doesn't extend the evaluation window past it. `data-export.js` gained 2 new cases (billing section present + correctly skipped when no individual billing customer exists) and its shared test mock gained `.in()`/`.maybeSingle()` support. Backend suite 1238 tests (+7 across the three areas), `lint:tooling`, `typecheck:functions` all green; migration verified as a byte-identical `CREATE OR REPLACE` before applying live.

- **2026-07-23 — SHIPPED: CSV import bridge for GPS/session-load data** (`docs/gps_wearable_csv_import_proposal.md` §1) — the smallest of the wearable-audit gaps, closed first since it reuses fully-working existing ingestion logic rather than needing new infrastructure.
  Extracted `session-load-import.js`'s core (provider-adapter dispatch, device<->athlete pairing lookup, the `callerWritableAthletes` authority gate, the idempotent `(user_id, session_id, provider)` upsert) into `utils/session-load-import-core.js` — the JSON endpoint became a thin wrapper around it, and the new `session-load-import-csv.js` (`POST /api/session-load-import/csv`) is a second thin wrapper that turns CSV text into `rows[]` (via `papaparse`) and calls the exact same core, so the two entry points can't drift (CLAUDE.md §4). This was a pure extraction — no behavior change to the existing JSON path, verified via new regression tests covering the paths that had **zero** prior test coverage (neither `session-load-import.js` nor its adapters had ever been tested before this pass).
  Added a `manual` adapter to `utils/session-load-adapters.js` for the raw "CSV numbers, no vendor export" case — a coach's own spreadsheet of typed-up training-log data, using FlagFit's own canonical column names (documented, with a few common aliases: `distance`/`total_distance_m`, `load`/`player_load`, etc.) rather than a dynamic column-mapping UI. A true arbitrary-column mapper (letting a coach keep their existing spreadsheet's own headers) is a real, separate gap for later if this fixed-name convention proves too rigid in practice — not attempted here.
  CSV arrives as JSON (`{provider, csv}`), not `multipart/form-data` — this codebase has no multipart parser anywhere and already has an established base64-in-JSON convention for file uploads (credential documents, TIER 1). Bounded to 2MB / 5000 rows so a bad upload can't hang a serverless invocation; malformed-row CSV parse errors surface in the same `failed[]` array as adapter-level failures (unpaired athlete, missing session id/timestamp) rather than a silent partial import.
  **Verification:** new tests for the `manual` adapter (4 cases), the refactored JSON endpoint (6 cases — unsupported provider, missing rows, successful pairing, unpaired-row surfacing, cross-athlete authority block, upsert-failure-doesn't-report-success), and the new CSV endpoint (6 cases — missing/empty/oversized CSV, unknown provider, successful manual-provider import, unpaired-row surfacing). `netlify.toml` redirect added for the new route. Backend suite 1254 tests (+16), `lint:tooling`, `typecheck:functions` all green. Garmin/Oura/WHOOP OAuth framework, the `/api/wearables/status` fix, and the Apple Health manual-export path (the proposal's remaining items) are tracked separately, not yet built.
  **Side-effect fix, `scripts/docs-regen.mjs`:** the extraction above (moving `session-load-import.js`'s real table access into `utils/session-load-import-core.js`) would have silently made the generated docs LESS accurate — the script only ever scanned `netlify/functions/*.js` top-level files, so `device_pairings`/`monitoring_providers` would have dropped off `session-load-import`'s "Touched by" list the moment that access moved into a utils file, a generalizable blind spot for any function that delegates its DB access to a shared module. Fixed by having the scanner also look one hop into each function's own local (`./...`) imports (deliberately not a full transitive walk — that pulled in unrelated tables through widely-shared low-level helpers like `base-handler.js`, false-positive noise this codebase doesn't need) and stripping comments before matching (a JSDoc usage-example line in `db-query-helper.js` and a comment in `consent-data-reader.js` were both matching as if they were real `.from()` calls). Net effect beyond this task: **surfaced a genuine, unrelated pre-existing bug** — `ai-chat.js` (via its imported `utils/smart-ai-service.js`) queries `conversation_summaries`, which is not a live table (confirmed against `live-schema.snapshot.json`); this was invisible before because `smart-ai-service.js` was never scanned. Flagged here, not fixed — out of scope for the payments/GPS work this pass is about; a real gap for whoever owns the AI chat feature next.

- **2026-07-23 — SHIPPED: real OAuth framework for Garmin/Oura/WHOOP (connect + callback + webhook), device_pairings token columns.** Continuation of `docs/gps_wearable_csv_import_proposal.md` §2, same "no credentials yet, build ready to wire up" constraint as the Stripe billing work.
  **Schema** (`20260723170000_wearable_oauth_token_columns.sql`): `device_pairings` gains `access_token_encrypted`/`refresh_token_encrypted`/`token_expires_at`/`scopes` — nullable, additive, existing GPS-vest CSV-import pairings (which don't use OAuth) are untouched.
  **`utils/token-crypto.js`** — AES-256-GCM envelope encryption for tokens before they ever reach the DB (key from `WEARABLE_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY`, lazy-loaded like `utils/stripe-client.js`'s Stripe client). **`utils/wearable-oauth-state.js`** — a signed, stateless CSRF `state` param (HMAC-SHA256 over `{userId, provider, nonce, iat}`, 10-minute TTL) rather than a new state-storage table; the state itself is the proof of who started the flow. **`utils/wearable-oauth-providers.js`** — one config object per vendor (same "add a provider = add a config" pattern as `utils/session-load-adapters.js`); every OAuth endpoint URL and client credential is read from an env var rather than hardcoded, since exact vendor endpoints (Garmin in particular has been migrating away from OAuth 1.0a) must be verified against each vendor's current developer docs before go-live, not asserted from memory. **`wearables-connect.js`**/**`wearables-callback.js`** implement the standard OAuth2 authorization-code flow end to end: redirect to consent screen with signed state → exchange code for tokens (`utils/wearable-oauth-client.js`, a mockable wrapper around the actual `fetch` calls, same principle as `utils/stripe-client.js`) → encrypt → upsert the pairing (handles the case where the vendor's token response carries no external athlete id at all, via a manual existing-row lookup since Postgres never treats two NULLs as equal for `ON CONFLICT`).
  **`wearables-webhook.js`** resolves `external_athlete_id → user_id` via `device_pairings` and ingests through `utils/wearable-health-ingest-core.js` — `wearable-health-ingest.js`'s core (consent gate + idempotent upsert) extracted the same way `session-load-import.js`'s was for the CSV bridge, so the athlete's own self-service ingest and the vendor's webhook push can't drift (CLAUDE.md §4). An unpaired external id or revoked consent both return 200 "ignored" rather than an error — neither is the vendor's mistake to retry, and erroring would just trigger endless redelivery.
  **Deliberately NOT built, flagged rather than guessed:** the webhook's request-body contract (`{externalAthleteId, readings: [...]}`) is this pass's own placeholder, not a verified Garmin/Oura/WHOOP spec — real vendor push-payload shapes (and in Garmin's case, whether the push even carries the reading vs. just a "new data available" ping requiring a follow-up fetch) need real vendor docs to get right, and building against a guessed shape with false confidence would be worse than flagging it plainly in the code and here. The token-refresh scheduled job (queued the same way `seat_sync_queue`'s drain job is — mechanism exists, no cron wired) is also not built.
  **A real bug found and fixed mid-build:** the webhook's success response originally spread `{received: true, ...result.data}`, but `result.data` (from the shared ingest core) already has its own `received` field — a COUNT of readings received, a number — which silently overwrote the boolean acknowledgment flag. Caught by the endpoint's own test before shipping; renamed the outer ack field to `acknowledged`.
  **Verification:** new tests across 8 files (43 cases) — `token-crypto` (round-trip, tamper detection via GCM auth tag, missing/wrong-length key), `wearable-oauth-state` (round-trip, wrong provider, tampered/wrong-secret signature, expiry, malformed), `wearable-oauth-providers` (registry, partial/full env-var config), `wearables-connect` (unknown/inactive provider, not-configured 503, correct redirect+state+URL), `wearables-callback` (missing code/state, vendor-denied, invalid/cross-provider state, successful upsert incl. the NULL-external-id fallback, token-exchange-failure doesn't save anything), `wearables-webhook` (unknown provider, missing/wrong signature, not-configured 503, unpaired/revoked-consent both 200-ignored, successful ingest), `wearable-health-ingest-core` (consent gate, failed-row surfacing, DB-error-doesn't-report-success), and a `wearable-health-ingest.js` regression suite proving its refactor onto the shared core changed nothing. `netlify.toml` redirects added for all 3 new routes. Security advisor re-run: no new findings against `device_pairings`. Backend suite 1297 tests (+43), `lint:tooling`, `typecheck:functions` all green.

- **2026-07-23 — SHIPPED: `GET /api/wearables/status` now reads real state instead of a hardcoded catalogue.** Follow-up to the OAuth framework above — the hardcoded 4-device array (`{id:"garmin",...connected:false}` etc., always false) was accurate the day it shipped (nothing could ever be connected) but became a real drift risk the moment `wearables-callback.js`/`wearables-webhook.js` gave `device_pairings` a genuine writer; left as-is it would have silently kept reporting every device disconnected forever, regardless of a real pairing.
  Rewrote `wearables.js` to derive the catalogue from `monitoring_providers` (`kind IN ('wearable','both')`, `is_active`), connection state from the caller's own `device_pairings` row (`is_active`), and `lastSync` from the most recent `wearable_health` reading per source (a bounded, ordered scan — PostgREST has no `GROUP BY`/aggregate select, so this reads up to 500 recent rows and takes the first occurrence per source rather than a true per-provider `MAX()`; fine at today's per-athlete volumes, a real limit if that ever needs a heavier data volume).
  **Corrected the device set itself, not just its data source:** the hardcoded list included `catapult`, a GPS-vest provider that's `external_load`-only in `monitoring_providers.kind` — never a wearable by the schema's own definition, and already served by the entirely separate `session-load-import`/CSV path. Dropped. It was also missing `oura` (real, seeded, `kind='wearable'`) entirely. Both were genuine mismatches between the hardcoded list and the schema it was meant to reflect, not stylistic choices.
  **No frontend change needed:** `device-data.component.ts`'s template already branches on `d.connected` correctly (`"connected"` vs `"sync coming"`) — it was written to handle a real connected state even though one could never occur before today. Its doc comment and `WearableService`'s both referenced the old "placeholder catalogue" framing; corrected in the same pass (CLAUDE.md §2) — they now correctly describe a real backend with no vendor credentials configured yet, not a placeholder.
  **Verification:** new `wearables-status-validation.test.js` (4 cases: catapult excluded/oura included, all-disconnected-when-unpaired, connected+lastSync-from-real-reading, disconnected-when-only-pairing-is-inactive) — zero prior coverage existed for this endpoint. Backend suite 1301 tests (+4), `lint:tooling`, `typecheck:functions` all green.

- **2026-07-23 — SHIPPED: Apple Health manual-export ingest, closing the last item in `docs/gps_wearable_csv_import_proposal.md`.** Apple has no server-to-server HealthKit API at all (deliberate Apple privacy design, not a gap engineering can close) — the only paths in are a native companion app (real, separate scope, not attempted) or a manual export, which this ships.
  **`wearable-health-ingest-apple-xml.js`** (`POST /api/wearable-health-ingest/apple-health-xml`) accepts the athlete's own `export.xml` (they export via the Health app's "Export All Health Data" and unzip the resulting `export.zip` themselves — no ZIP-extraction dependency added here) as raw text in a JSON body, same base64/raw-text-in-JSON convention as the CSV bridge. Bounded to 5MB — a full lifetime export can be far larger; that needs a chunked/background import this single request/response cycle doesn't support, a real flagged gap, not attempted.
  **`utils/apple-health-xml.js`** parses `<Record>` elements with a small, dependency-free regex extractor rather than pulling in a full XML-parsing library — Apple's export format for `<Record>` is a simple, flat, self-closing-tag shape, a real and stable structure (unlike a vendor webhook payload), so this doesn't carry the same false-confidence risk flagged for the OAuth webhook contract above. Maps 8 `HKQuantityTypeIdentifier` types relevant to load/recovery monitoring (heart rate, resting heart rate, HRV, distance walking/running, active energy, steps, VO2 max, respiratory rate) to canonical `wearable_health.metric` names — there's no existing metric-name vocabulary to conflict with, since nothing has ever consumed `wearable_health` data yet (confirmed: this whole ingest lane is still ORPHANED, no dashboard reads it). **Category types (e.g. `HKCategoryTypeIdentifierSleepAnalysis`) are deliberately not mapped** — their `value` is a state name, not a number, and a real "hours slept" figure needs aggregating potentially many overlapping asleep-state intervals per night; a real, separate gap, flagged rather than approximated.
  Ingests through the same `utils/wearable-health-ingest-core.js` the OAuth webhook uses (`source: "apple_health"`, already a seeded `monitoring_providers`/`wearable_consent` key) — same consent gate, same idempotent upsert, three entry points (self-service JSON, vendor webhook, Apple manual-export) now share one ingestion implementation (CLAUDE.md §4).
  **Verification:** `apple-health-xml.test.js` (7 cases: quantity-type mapping, multiple types in one export, category-type skip, non-numeric-value skip, XML-entity unescaping, truncation at a reading cap while still reporting the true record count, zero-record export) and `wearable-health-ingest-apple-xml-validation.test.js` (6 cases: missing/oversized xml, no-Records-found, records-but-none-importable, consent-blocked, successful ingest). `netlify.toml` redirect added. Backend suite 1314 tests (+13), `lint:tooling`, `typecheck:functions` all green.
  **GPS/wearable proposal now fully closed:** CSV import bridge (§1, incl. the `manual` provider for raw-numbers spreadsheets), Garmin/Oura/WHOOP OAuth framework (§2), and Apple Health manual export (§3) are all built. Remaining, explicitly flagged, not-built gaps across all three: real vendor credentials (none configured — nothing processes real data yet, per the standing instruction this work was scoped under), the webhook's placeholder request-body contract (needs real vendor docs), the token-refresh scheduled job, a dynamic CSV column-mapping UI, sleep-analysis aggregation, and full-history (>5MB/>5000-row) imports for either CSV or Apple Health.

- **2026-07-23 — AUDIT: 4 Stripe billing endpoints had no `netlify.toml` redirect since the payments work shipped earlier this session.** A final pass verifying every endpoint added this session (payments + GPS/wearable) actually has a redirect — the exact class of gap this file's own 2026-07-22 entry (Bug 3) flagged in a different feature — found `stripe-checkout.js`, `stripe-webhook.js`, `stripe-portal.js`, and `billing-lapse-check.js` all fully built and tested, but 404'd in production: none had a `[[redirects]]` block. Fixed: `/api/billing/checkout` → `stripe-checkout`, `/api/billing/webhook` → `stripe-webhook`, `/api/billing/portal` → `stripe-portal`, `/api/billing/lapse-check` → `billing-lapse-check`. The 9 GPS/wearable endpoints added this session (`session-load-import-csv`, `wearables-connect/-callback/-webhook`, `wearable-health-ingest-apple-xml`, plus the payments-side `stripe-*`/`billing-lapse-check` above) were all cross-checked the same way — no other gaps found. Backend suite 1314 tests (unchanged — a redirect-only fix, no code path changed), `lint:tooling`, `typecheck:functions` all green.

- **2026-07-23 — SHIPPED: replaced the permanent Free tier with a 7-day full-access trial + hard paywall (`utils/entitlements.js` rewrite), plus full Angular billing UI.** Direct product decisions from the club owner: *"Billable seat is every person who gets in"* (confirms the existing default — every active `team_members` row); *"Yes. There is no voluntary pause. And no discounts sad to see you go"* (billing continues through any voluntary pause; no retention discount on cancel); *"Free tier is only for a week, to get a glimpse of it"* (the big one — replaces the permanent Free tier entirely); the 3-year retention default and dropping manual/offline fee tracking were both confirmed/decided the same way (see `docs/payments_billing_and_data_retention_proposal.md` §9, now RESOLVED).
  **Blast-radius catch before shipping:** anchoring the trial on `users.created_at` would have retroactively locked out 12 of 13 real pilot users (oldest signed up 137 days ago) the instant this went live. Fixed by adding `users.trial_started_at` (`20260723180000_add_trial_started_at.sql`, `DEFAULT now()`) as a column separate from `created_at` — all existing rows backfilled to one synchronized "fresh start" timestamp instead of a per-row historical date.
  **`utils/entitlements.js`:** `TRIAL_LIMITS` (everything unlocked — `historyDays`/`maxTeams`/`maxAthletesPerRoster`/`maxClients` all `null`, export/wearable-sync/roster-management all `true`) applies for `TRIAL_DAYS=7` from `trial_started_at`. Once elapsed with no paid subscription, `LOCKED_LIMITS` (all zero/false) applies and `entitlement.locked === true`. Priority order, in the rewrite: active paid access (individual not-suspended OR team) beats everything; a past-due-suspended individual locks regardless of any trial state (never falls back to "still on trial"); only then does trial-active/trial-expired get evaluated. A subtle pre-existing nuance was preserved with a dedicated regression test: when a team subscription covers an individual whose own subscription is separately `past_due`, the individual's own status is NOT silently overwritten by the team's `active` status (`locked: false` but `status: "past_due"` stays visible).
  **`GET /api/billing/status`** (`billing-status.js`, new) returns `{tier, status, locked, trialDaysRemaining, appliedTiers, limits, subscription, hasIndividualBillingCustomer}` — the one place the frontend reads entitlement state from.
  **Hard paywall gate**, representative pattern in `training-sessions.js`/`daily-load.js`: the lock check runs AFTER the endpoint's own query-param validation (in `training-sessions.js` specifically — validation can throw its own 422 first), not before — a malformed request is a client mistake independent of billing state, and shouldn't be preempted by the paywall gate. Returns HTTP 402 `subscription_required` when locked.
  **Minors + billing:** `stripe-checkout.js` blocks individual (non-team) tier checkout for accounts under 18 (`ageFromDob()` on `date_of_birth ?? birth_date`, reusing the same dual-column convention as `cohort.js`/`player-settings.js`; 403 `minor_account`). Team Package checkout is unaffected — team billing never touches an individual's card, so a minor on a team roster still gets full Team Package coverage.
  **Angular frontend (previously zero — nothing existed for billing or wearable OAuth in the UI):** `BillingService` (`core/services/billing.service.ts` — status/checkout/portal, `firstValueFrom` + `LoggerService` pattern, checkout/portal return a URL string in JSON that the frontend navigates to itself, same reasoning as the wearable-connect fix below); `PricingComponent` (`/billing`, lazy, real prices from `docs/legal/BUSINESS_PLAN_SUBSCRIPTIONS.md` §2.1 — not placeholders; team-tier purchase resolves `teamId` from `TeamMembershipService`, blocked client-side for non-owner/admin); `PaywallComponent` (`/paywall`, full-screen, deliberately OUTSIDE the Shell — no bottom nav, since a locked account shouldn't present the rest of the app as reachable; distinct copy for `suspended` vs plain trial-expired); `billingGuard` (`canActivateChild` on the Shell route, not `canActivate` — the latter only fires once on first entry into a subtree, the former re-fires on every in-app navigation within the already-loaded Shell, which is what a paywall actually needs; exempts `/settings`, `/billing`, `/paywall` so a locked user can still reach the screens that let them pay); Settings → new Billing tab (plan name, trial countdown or locked/suspended message, renewal date, "See all plans" link, "Manage billing" → Stripe Portal, lazy-loaded only when the tab is actually opened).
  **Verification:** 13 entitlements unit tests (was a full rewrite of the suite), full backend suite green, 8 new Angular component/service/guard test files (`billing.service.spec.ts`, `wearable.service.spec.ts`, `pricing.component.spec.ts`, `paywall.component.spec.ts`, `billing.guard.spec.ts`, `settings.component.spec.ts`, plus `device-data.component.spec.ts` updates below), Angular suite 836 tests green, `tsc --noEmit`/`eslint` clean on both sides. `ng build` itself couldn't run in this environment (Node v22.22.2 vs the Angular CLI's required v22.22.3+, no alternate Node install available) — `tsc --noEmit` with `strictTemplates: true` (confirmed in `tsconfig.json`, which fully type-checks templates, not just component TS) plus the full Angular vitest suite substituted as the correctness gate; flagged explicitly rather than silently skipped.

- **2026-07-23 — SHIPPED: fixed `wearables-connect.js`'s auth architecture bug, built the real wearable Connect/consent/Apple-Health UI.** Found while wiring the Connect button up to the real frontend for the first time (task-level integration testing catches what unit tests of either side alone can't): `wearables-connect.js` returned a raw HTTP 302 redirect while requiring a Bearer `Authorization` header — but this SPA's auth can only attach that header via `HttpClient` + an interceptor, never via a plain `<a href>` top-level browser navigation (no cookies in this auth model). Fixed to return JSON (`createSuccessResponse({authorizeUrl})`) instead of a redirect, mirroring the already-working `stripe-checkout.js`/`stripe-portal.js` pattern: authenticated XHR call returns a URL in the response body, the frontend does `window.location.href = url` itself.
  **`wearables.js`** (`GET /api/wearables/status`) additionally returns each device's `consentState` (`'granted'|'revoked'|null`, read from `wearable_consent`) alongside the existing `connected`/`pairedAt`/`lastSync` — the UI needs this to render a real Grant/Revoke-sync toggle rather than just a connected/disconnected flag.
  **`device-data.component.ts`** gained: a real Connect button per unpaired device (calls `WearableService.connect(providerKey)`, navigates to the resolved vendor authorize URL); a Grant-sync/Revoke-sync button per connected device (calls the existing `wearable-health-ingest.js` PUT consent endpoint); a banner reading the OAuth callback's `?connected=<provider>` return query param (`wearables-callback.js` already redirected to `/device-data?connected=<provider>`, but nothing read it before); an Apple Health `export.xml` upload control (file picker → read as text → POST to `wearable-health-ingest-apple-xml.js`, surfacing the backend's real validation message verbatim on failure — unlike billing errors, which stay generic, this one tells the athlete exactly what to fix: "no `<Record>` elements found", "consent required", etc.).
  **Verification:** rewrote the `wearables-connect-validation.test.js` redirect-assertion test to expect the new 200+JSON shape; added 2 new `wearables-status-validation.test.js` cases for `consentState`; 8 new `device-data.component.spec.ts` cases (Connect navigation, connect-error handling, the `?connected=` banner, consent toggling, Apple Health upload success/failure) — jsdom in this environment doesn't allow redefining `window.location`, so the navigation test asserts the pre-navigation state (provider requested, no error, busy flag left set since the page is about to leave) rather than the literal `href` write. Full backend + Angular suites, `lint:tooling`, `tsc --noEmit` all green.

## 7. Runbooks & Security (operational — folded from the old RUNBOOKS, stale traps fixed)

Canonical: health = `/api/health`; env vars = `SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY` (primary) **or** `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` (fallback) — code checks both via `||` in `supabase-client.js`; set either one; project ref `grfjmnjpzvknmsxrwesx`. `workout_logs` no longer exists — never reference it in ops queries.

**Health / triage.** `curl -s https://<site>/api/health | jq` → expect `{status:"healthy"}`; `degraded`=investigate, `unhealthy`=incident. Logs: `netlify logs:function <name> --last 50`, `netlify logs:build`. Severity: SEV-1 outage <15m, SEV-2 major feature <1h, SEV-3 degraded <4h, SEV-4 cosmetic next-day.

**Deployment rollback.** Fast: `netlify rollback` → re-check `/api/health`. Specific: `netlify deploys --json | jq '.[:10]'` → `netlify deploys:publish <ID>`. Code: `git revert <hash> --no-edit && git push origin main`. Rule: cause unclear after 5–15m → roll back. **Migrations are forward-only** (no down migrations); for DB-caused regressions restore from a pre-migration dump, not `supabase migration down`.

**Backup / restore.** Pre-migration: `supabase db dump -f backups/pre_migration_$(date ...).sql`. Record row counts before/after for `users`, `training_sessions`, `player_programs`. Restore full: `psql "<conn>" < file.sql` (destructive); selective: `SET session_replication_role='replica';` → restore → `'origin'`. Env: `netlify env:list` (encrypted — has secrets); PITR is Supabase Pro-only; Storage buckets are NOT auto-backed-up.

**Account deletion.** request → soft delete (immediate, sessions revoked) → 30-day cancellable queue → hard delete. API `account-deletion.js` (`/api/account-deletion`); edge fn `supabase/functions/process-deletions` (pg_cron 3 AM); DB fns `initiate_account_deletion()`, `cancel_account_deletion()`, `process_hard_deletion(uuid)`, `get_deletions_ready_for_processing()`; tables `account_deletion_requests`, `privacy_audit_log`. Backlog: `SELECT count(*) FROM account_deletion_requests WHERE status='pending' AND scheduled_hard_delete_at<=now();` (warn>10, crit>50). Force: `SELECT process_hard_deletion('<uuid>');`. Auth cleanup: `supabase.auth.admin.deleteUser('<uuid>')`.

**Privacy incident.** GDPR: notify DPA within 72h of a high/medium-risk breach. Containment: rotate Netlify + `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, invalidate sessions, `UPDATE users SET is_active=false WHERE id='<suspect>'`. Correct live columns: `consent_access_log(user_id, accessed_by, access_type, data_category, accessed_at, reason, consent_given)`; `parental_consent` keyed by `minor_user_id`, gate on `status`/`verified_at`. AI opt-out check: `ai_chat_sessions` × `privacy_settings WHERE ai_processing_enabled=false`. Validate: `npm run test:privacy`.

**Auth hardening.** Native HIBP leaked-password check is a GoTrue setting (Dashboard → Auth → Providers → Email, or Management API `PATCH /v1/projects/<ref>/config/auth {"password_hibp_enabled":true}`) — NOT toggleable via SQL; the app-level edge fn does not clear the advisor. **Fixed 2026-07-07/08:** the ~10 SECURITY DEFINER RLS-helper fns (`ff_is_active_team_member`, `ff_is_team_staff`, `has_role`, `is_active_superadmin`, `auth_user_team_ids`, `can_view_player_performance`, …) were `anon`-executable via a leftover `PUBLIC` grant — `REVOKE ... FROM public, anon` was applied to all of them (`authenticated`/`service_role` keep direct grants, which RLS calls rely on). Verified live: `anon`-executable SECURITY DEFINER count = **0**. Any NEW definer fn → `REVOKE FROM public, anon; GRANT authenticated, service_role` from the start — do not rely on a bare PUBLIC default.

---

## 8. Contributing — the Ledger is the contract

**Any PR that adds or changes a table, endpoint, or feature MUST update §4 (Feature Status Ledger) in the same commit.** This is how we stop rebuilding what exists.

**Regenerating the generated sections** (after a schema or route change):

1. Refresh the live schema snapshot: re-run the Supabase introspection (via Supabase MCP `execute_sql` over `information_schema`) and overwrite `docs/generated/live-schema.snapshot.json` (keep its shape; bump `generatedOn`). Optionally regenerate `supabase-types.ts` too.
2. `npm run docs:regen` — rewrites `docs/generated/DATA_MODEL.md` + `ENDPOINTS.md` deterministically and re-stamps the date.
3. Commit the regenerated files with your change.

If a generated file shows new ⚠️ ghost-table refs or DRIFT, fix the cause or log it in §6 — do not hand-edit the generated files (they are overwritten).
